candidates.stmt:
Born and Raised in Addlestone - I am glad to serve as the current Surrey County & Runnymede Borough Councillor for Addlestone (August 2025) supporting the community that I deeply care about. Over the past year I have worked tirelessly to ensure residents are properly represented and that their voices are heard in decisions that affect our area.

Alongside my role as your double-hatter councillor, I work in the Aviation and Aerospace logistics sector, leading business development for specialist freight movements. My professional background gives me a strong understanding of the challenges faced by businesses, workers and families and highlights the importance of services and practical, results driven decision-making.

Since being elected, I have worked closely with residents, local organisations and community groups to deliver tangible improvements across Addlestone, from supporting projects in Victory Park and collaborating with local clubs and volunteer groups to raising concerns about roads, infrastructure and local services. My Focus always has and always will be, on delivering positive & Visible results for our community.

Your Support and vote will ensure that residents continue to see progress and meaningful change in the next five years.

Its a genuine privilege to represent Addlestone & I remain committed to working hard for our residents and building a brighter future for our community.

Your Voice. Your Streets. Your Addlestone.

My family's connection to Addlestone spans generations, dating back to my great grandfather building his home here in the early 1900s. I've been deeply involved in our community for many years, managing the local Community Centre and supporting diverse activities for residents of all ages. These experiences, coupled with regular conversations, have given me a profound understanding of the issues that matter most and the vital importance of protecting our cherished community facilities.
I am standing as your local Councillor to be a strong, clear voice for residents, advocating for practical improvements that will make Addlestone a safe, welcoming, and thriving town.
Transparency in how local council funds are spent is essential, and I firmly believe these resources must be utilized wisely and responsibly for the benefit of all.
My key priorities include tackling anti-social behaviour head-on and revitalizing areas that have suffered from neglect or deterioration. I am passionate about restoring Addlestone's beauty and safety, aiming to make it as vibrant and welcoming as it was when my great grandfather first called this place home.
A strong local voice translates into effective local action. I am wholeheartedly committed to working tirelessly for our community's future, ensuring Addlestone remains a place we can all be proud to call home.

A 30-year Addlestone resident, I combine senior Financial Services leadership with deep experience in youth services volunteering, and neurodiversity and disability advocacy for West Surrey. As a candidate who is severely visually impaired, I offer a unique perspective on accessibility and inclusion.
I am committed to revitalising Addlestone’s High Street through policies like one-hour free parking and removing green waste charges. To support our significant youth population, I have petitioned to save Addlestone’s Family Hub. By securing notional rent for third sector entities, we can restore vital youth services and ensure sports funding reaches the heart of our community.

I grew up locally and now live in Addlestone with my family. I run a business in Weybridge and have spent my career managing teams, solving problems, and delivering services that people rely on.

Through my own family experience, I’ve seen first-hand how important strong, reliable local services are. When those services work, they make life easier. When they don’t, people feel it straight away.

I’m standing because I believe local decisions should be practical, fair, and focused on what actually improves people’s day-to-day lives, not driven by politics or short-term thinking.

My focus is simple:

* Making sure local services are consistent, accessible, and accountable
* Supporting families who rely on those services the most
* Bringing a practical, real-world approach to decision-making

I’m not a career politician. I’m someone who understands how things work on the ground, at home, at work, and in the community and I’ll bring that same mindset into the role.

If elected, I’ll focus on doing the job properly, being visible, and making decisions that genuinely serve the people who live here.

Jenny has been a resident in Addlestone for over 20 years and has fought for accessible local healthcare, a cleaner local environment and more action to fix our crumbling roads and highways after years of neglect. She hopes to join the strong Liberal Democrat team at County Hall - shaping the future of Surrey as the Government starts redrawing its council borders.

Speaking about the upcoming election, she said “Addlestone has seen many changes since I first moved here, but local politics remains very one-sided. I'd like our Council to better reflect the diversity of viewpoints, and encourage residents to be involved in the future of their neighbourhood.”

I have lived in Addlestone for over 15 years. This is my home and where I am raising my family. I love this area but I believe it can and will be better. This is my community, I believe in it and I will fight for it.

I will be a Councillor for all, not just Labour voters. I will always be honest, fair and transparent with constituents. Trust in politics has been eroded over recent years and I am committed to playing my part in building up that vital trust again.

I do not take any vote for granted. If you choose to support me on the 21st August, I promise I will give it my all to repay the trust you’ve placed in me.

Residents want change. The Tories have failed us in Addlestone.  It’s now a choice between progress with Labour, or rolling the dice with Reform.

m a Pharmacist, mother of two, and an active
member of my children's school’s PTA. I have seen how austerity has impacted our local
area and I want to be able to give something
back to the community and support residents.

I will be a Councillor for all, not just Labour voters. I will always be honest, fair and transparent with constituents. Trust in politics has been eroded over recent years and I am committed to playing my part in building up that vital trust again.

I do not take any vote for granted. If you choose to support me on the 21st August, I promise I will give it my all to repay the trust you’ve placed in me.

Residents want change. The Tories have failed us in Addlestone.  It’s now a choice between progress with Labour, or rolling the dice with Reform.

Together, change is possible.

Carla Morson is a long-standing Ash Vale resident dedicated to building a strong, supportive community for everyone. She has a proven record of helping local people, including through her work with the local foodbank, and continues to stand up for residents who need support.

Alongside her community work, Carla has volunteered with Citizens Advice, gaining first-hand understanding of the challenges facing individuals and families. She was elected as County Councillor for Ash and Parish Councillor for Ash South in 2021, and in 2023 was elected as Borough Councillor for Ash Vale.

Carla is campaigning to improve services for young people, older residents, and vulnerable groups. She is committed to protecting local green spaces, tackling environmental issues, and ensuring sustainable development. Her priorities also include better infrastructure, improved GP access, enhanced public transport, and safer roads—delivering the investment and services Ash and Ash Vale deserves.

I am standing because I care deeply about Ash and the people who live here. For over ten years, I have served as a local Parish Councillor, working closely with residents to strengthen our community and tackle the issues that matter most.
I have a strong commitment to community life and enjoy planning and organising parish events that bring residents together. I believe strong communities are built on involvement, pride, and practical action.
My priorities are repairing our local roads, engaging constructively on development, improving local transport, including step‑free access at Ash Vale station, and securing a fairer deal for Surrey residents on council tax. I want hard‑working residents to get better value for the money they pay.
I am standing to deliver practical, workable improvements that promote pride, wellbeing, and make Ash an even better place to live.

I'm Freya and I'm standing as your Green Party candidate. I came to Surrey for work and it quickly became home. The place itself is beautiful and the sense of community here is something I want to protect and strengthen for everyone who lives here.

By day I'm a flavour chemist and outside of work one of my greatest passions is aerial arts which I also teach in Haslemere. I'm a supporter of Cats Protection, Greenpeace and the Surrey Wildlife Trust and those values of care for animals and our natural world run through everything I believe in politically.

I believe the Green Party stands for something more than the politics of the day. It refuses to cling to a status quo that is leaving our communities behind and wants to build a stronger foundation from which something new can grow, bringing us together rather than dividing us, whatever our differences.

**What I will fight for**

If elected I will:

- Push for more social housing with sustainable features and developments that genuinely work for the communities they are built in
- Make sure consultations with local people are properly heard and that developers stick to their agreements
- Take on the privatised water companies. The River Wey has seen sewage dumps nearly one hundred times lasting over one thousand hours. That is shameful and I will hold water companies and sewage works to account
- Monitor our waterways and fight for clean rivers for everyone in Surrey

Hello, my name is Josh and I believe we need a common-sense approach to how Ash is run. My background is rooted in a passion for wildlife and a drive to fix the socio-economic issues that often get ignored. I am not all about party politics — I am interested in getting things right for this community.

**Protecting Green Belt Land**

Ash is under constant pressure from developers. I will firmly oppose any building on Green Belt. These spaces are the _lungs_ of the village and once they are gone, we cannot get them back. I want to see local parks and Parish-managed land turned into proper nature corridors, using wildflower planting and better land management to bring biodiversity back to the heart of Ash.

**Housing that Works for Locals**

When developers try to build here, I will push for greener social housing. We need homes that are actually affordable to heat and built to last, rather than just hitting a quota. I will be a loud voice for ensuring any new projects include the infrastructure and green space that Ash actually needs.

**If elected, I would focus on:**
- Keeping council tax money in the local economy by using local labour rather than expensive outside contractors
- Lobbying the County Council to ensure families get the SEND support their children deserve
- Making sure repairs and upkeep across the village are done to a standard we can all be proud of

I am offering a calm, practical and green perspective for Ash. I would be proud to represent you and make sure the village stays a fair and green place to live.

"I have lived in Spelthorne since 1968 and I have seen this borough transform, I attended The Laurels School in Clarendon Road Ashford where my children also attended, they then moved to Senior School in the borough and still reside here.

I work full time as a Procurement Manager purchasing food and packaging.

I became a Councillor in 2011 and have met many residents' concerns and delivered solutions to improve the quality of their lives and improve local surroundings.  I project led the Dog Fouling campaign and lobbied South West Trains and Network rail along with our MP to refurbish Ashford Train Station.  Liaised with residents on the replanting of Scott Freeman Gardens and initiated Ashford North Resident Association (ANRA) giving the residents a stronger presence.

I look forward to continuing to serve and support my residents in Ashford and Stanwell for the next four years."

Chris has lived in Spelthorne for over 60 years, a retired Facilities Manager who has extensive experience in management. Chris still volunteers, working with young and vulnerable people.
Being a Christian, Chris tries to apply Christian values to his life. He is a pragmatist and believes in Rights and Respect for each other, but most importantly believes in taking Responsibility.
Chris is prepared to ask and debate issues that the Council and the current parties shy away from and believes in open and honest dialogue.

Paul Woodward is the Conservative candidate at the Ashford Town by-election on Wednesday 6 September.

This election is taking place because a councillor who was elected as a Green Party candidate in May quit just a few weeks later, meaning the Spelthorne taxpayer must now fund a by-election.

Ashford needs a councillor who is committed to our community and fighting for local residents at Spelthorne Borough Council – that person is Paul Woodward.

Paul has lived in Ashford Town for 34 years and is the ONLY candidate at this election to live in the ward.

A vote for Paul and the Conservatives will also help send a message to Labour’s Mayor of London Sadiq Khan to rethink his damaging ULEZ plans. Don't forget, Sadiq Khan’s Ultra Low Emission Zone is being extended to our border this month. It means that from 29 August any of our residents or businesses who cross over into London boroughs in an older or diesel car may be forced to pay the Mayor £12.50 per day.

This is an extremely ill-conceived and badly timed tax on poorer families during a time of high inflation, yet it enjoys the support of Labour, Greens and the Lib Dems at City Hall. Conservative councils are fighting against ULEZ and your support for Paul Woodward at this election can strengthen their opposition.

Paul has bags of experience as a past councillor. He has also worked in IT for top firms including British Airways and Lloyds Bank. He will be a tireless champion for Ashford, working closely with our pro-active serving Conservative councillor Olivia Rybinski.

Please use your vote on 6 September to elect Paul Woodward, a LOCAL champion for Ashford.

Manu is a local business owner and has worked as a scientist in the field of neuroscience. Manu spent much of his youth in and around Spelthorne, his father worked here as a taxi driver for several decades and growing up Manu spent a great deal of time on Church Road in Ashford – seeing first-hand how vital accessible local services are to everyday life.

Satpal works in a senior role in logistics at nearby Heathrow Airport and has lived in Ashford for many years with his family. This experience makes him well placed to understand the needs of our community. Satpal believes that Ashford has been neglected by the Conservatives for far too long and will be a fantastic local champion for our town on the new Unitary Authority.

Richard was born near Hastings on the south coast of England where a passion for the natural world at an early age led to him studying geology at university and to a career as a geoscientist in the global oil and gas industry. He now lives with his family on the outskirts of Chobham village, just to the north of Woking.

New to politics, Richard finally made the decision to stand once the gradual morphing of the two-party system into a single mindset became palpable. Uncontrolled mass immigration, high taxation, low growth and big state/state interference are now endorsed on both sides of the political divide.

Furthermore, a total failure to address the ‘woke’ agenda which has taken root across society, eroding freedom of speech, re-writing our history, threatening our culture - indeed, our very identity as a nation - can only be interpreted as endorsement of its objectives. Add a ‘coalition of groupthink’ on Net Zero to the mix and incalculable economic damage is inevitable without a rapid change of direction.

Richard is a staunch advocate of informed decision-making based on free, open and honest debate, supported by all available data and ensuring the full spectrum of views are heard. This is the path to wisdom, enabling humanity to address the great issues of our time. He is also a great believer in good governance, recognising that reform of many public institutions is needed to ensure authority is vested appropriately, with effective oversight, to minimise unfavourable outcomes - Woking Borough Council’s bankruptcy being a case in point.

He is now standing for Reform UK to provide the people of Woking with a genuine alternative to the establishment parties; a party with an aspirational vision based on common sense and British values.

If you, too, seek real change, join us, volunteer and help us campaign!

I’ve lived and worked in Berkshire and Surrey since the 1980s, and I moved to Camberley in 2010. My family’s connections to Camberley go back to the early 1900s. I began my career in Social Services, supporting communities facing multiple deprivation and mental health challenges, before moving into the commercial sector. Over the years I’ve negotiated major contracts with and for government organisations worldwide and helped organisations deliver on Corporate Social Responsibility. Now that I’m retired, I focus full time on doing everything I can to make life better locally. That’s why I’ve taken on the work involved in being:
•	County Councillor for Camberley East
•	Community representative to the NHS
•	Chair of three charities: Old Dean Community Group CIO; Surrey Tree Warden Network; and Surrey Heath Tree Wardens
When the pandemic hit, I set up and ran the Free Food Stall to support residents who were struggling. That work continues today because Old Dean remains an area of multiple deprivation, with many people facing overlapping challenges.
I’m also known for persistent lobbying for road improvements and for making sure potholes in Camberley East are dealt with promptly earning me the nickname “The Pothole Man”.
Outside my council and community work, I campaign for stronger protection for nature and continue developing my wildlife photography. My priorities are:
•	Supporting the most vulnerable
•	Improving access to joined-up health and care
•	Securing investment in a new hospital
•	Stopping harmful developments that threaten our environment, while supporting appropriate growth
•	Making roads safer and keeping them in good condition to support our local economy
•	Backing local businesses

I have lived in Old Dean for 22 years. Living within the community where I am standing, I truly understand our local needs. I live here with my wife, our two children, and our Cockapoo.
As a parent of neurodiverse children, I am a dedicated advocate for families. For over 15 years, I have served as a school governor, currently supporting three local primary schools and a secondary school. I am also a trustee for three local charities. Having served as a Borough Councillor for nearly seven years, I have a proven track record of delivering results for our community.

I have lived and worked in Surrey Heath for over 30 years.   Born in Farnham, Surrey in the 1960s I been fortunate to travel the World as my father was in the British Army.  After 10 years of living in London for work reasons, I settled in Bagshot as my home to raise my three now grown up children.

During their time growing up I was passionate about their development and became involved with local issues.  I was Chair of the Bagshot Playing Fields Association in the late 1990s and committed to saving green spaces and keeping open leisure activities free to the local community.  I was also involved with the Guides and Scouts, the local Football Club, Curley Park Rangers, and my passion for the local schools.  For the past 14 years I have worked as a Governor and now a Trustee of five of Surrey Heath’s most successful state schools.

I joined the Brexit Party in 2019 as I realised I was politically homeless.  If I wanted to make changes locally then I had to stand up and be counted.  With Brexit still not secured by the current Government I have joined Reform Party UK to keep fighting for those British values we need to protect.

I believe funding for local areas should be given to local councils as they know best where to spend their money, not from Westminster down.  The current Government have no idea what it is like to survive financially in the current climate or high taxes, low incomes and rising costs of living.  Local councils need to step up to what is really needed and they too need a shake up to bring politics into the modern world for the people who live in their local areas.

The crumbling NHS has been and still is mismanaged.  People are dying in corridors as there are not enough employees available to work.  We heralded the Doctors and Nurses in the Covid era yet we have done nothing to show how much we value them.

Our local schools are run in buildings that should have been knocked down years ago, there is again no money available for renovating or r

I am standing as a Green Party candidate, to offer a strong, reliable, voice for our community. I have lived within the area for most of my life and want to work in strengthening and unifying our community.

Our area deserves safe roads free from pot holes, and I mean roads that stay free from pot holes focusing on sustainable solutions instead of fast fixes that don’t last. I’d also work on improving career and mental health support available.

I also believe there should be more free community spaces within our town, where people can socialise and get to know their neighbours better. There’s so many empty spaces within the town centre that could be better used whilst waiting for new residency. Places that provide spaces to do arts and crafts sessions and play board games. The community building opportunities are endless.

I’d bring a breadth of experience of community engagement, youth work, mental health and wellness. As well as utilising my skills as a graphic designer and animator to be a more creative community.

If elected, I will work hard for you, your family and our community, and most importantly bring more joy to the community.

Having grown up in a household with profoundly deaf parents, I have a strong sense of caring for the wider
community so that everyone can take part. I care deeply about rebuilding public services and protecting the
vulnerable.

My working background until retirement was in accountancy, taxation and internal audit. I would work
with all parties at county level to ensure that local government reorganisation proceeds smoothly.

Toni Hourahane has 3 grown sons, a beautiful daughter-in-law and a grandson.  She has been been an active member of her Guildford church for 25 years. 

Given the opportunity to serve Clandon and Horsley, she wants to make sure that the countryside remains naturally green and the historic town of Guildford keeps its identity. Her honours year of her BSc as a mature student, was spent studying sustainability and learning how to help this planet.  She has run a successful local computer consultancy for 25 years.  She was part of a  team of volunteers who managed the delivery of 2000 vaccines a day including updating certificates for travel.  She has volunteered for a local charity for the visually impaired and those children suffering from hearing loss for 7 years.

Caring & Campaigning for our Community

I’m passionate about people and where we live. Concerned about traffic congestion and striving to make journeys safer, economical and environmentally friendly without spoiling the landscape. A local resident of 31 years, I’ve been a school governor, scout leader and Armed Forces Champion and a councillor 2012-16. 2023-2027.
Past Mayor of Runnymede.

Born and raised just across the bridge in
Spelthorne and educated in Chertsey, my roots
in this community run deep. For nearly a
decade, I have proudly called Runnymede my
home, providing me with a firsthand
understanding of the unique challenges and
opportunities facing our area.
Currently serving as the Reform UK councillor
for Addlestone South, I am honored to act as a
firm voice for our residents. My role involves
more than just attending meetings; it is about
actively challenging decisions that fail our
community and championing the values that
local people hold dear. I believe that effective
representation requires a genuine connection to
the streets and neighborhoods one serves.
Through my professional background and
personal dedication, I have built strong ties
across the constituency. I am a committed
advocate for practical solutions, ensuring that
local government remains accountable and
transparent. My track record demonstrates an
unwavering commitment to the people and
places that make our region special.
As your Reform UK candidate for Chertsey, I
promise to bring this same energy and local
expertise to the table. I am determined to
ensure our town is no longer overlooked,
providing the dedicated, common-sense
representation Chertsey deserves.

Liz Townsend is standing for election to the new West Surrey Council in May 2026 to continue delivering for Cranleigh and Ewhurst.

A well-known local representative, Liz has worked year-round to support residents and get results on the issues that matter most. She has helped families with children with additional needs, including setting up a local support group, and has held Thames Water to account over leaks and sewage dumping affecting the area.

She has also delivered a range of practical local improvements, including upgrading Lashmere Play Park, resurfacing Barhatch Lane and other rural roads, installing new bus shelters, and introducing traffic calming measures on Elmbridge Road, alongside new vehicle-activated speed signs on Horsham Road and Ewhurst Road in Cranleigh and Cranleigh Road in Ewhurst.

Liz has led major local investment projects, including the new energy-efficient leisure centre and skatepark, helping to improve facilities for residents of all ages.
Through her work across all levels of local government, Liz has ensured that Cranleigh and Ewhurst secure more of the funding and support they need.

At Waverley Borough Council, she has led key strategies to support local businesses and strengthen environmental action. This includes reducing pesticide use and introducing stronger sustainability standards for council housing.

If elected to West Surrey Council, Liz will focus on improving access to local health and community services, including building on initiatives such as the Hospital Hoppa service she helped establish, tackling traffic and infrastructure pressures in Cranleigh and Ewhurst, supporting families of children with additional needs, and securing more support for carers. She will also continue to hold water companies to account while investing in greener, more sustainable local facilities.

I am standing because I believe we have a real chance to change the way our new West Surrey Unitary council is run and to make decisions that think longer term about the things that matter most.

I grew up in Cranleigh and Ewhurst and was outside all the time as a child. The Surrey Hills and North Downs shaped me as a person and I have a very deep connection to this land. Now I am grateful to be bringing my own children up here and to give them the same access to those natural spaces on their doorstep. That is something I feel a deep responsibility to protect.

The state of our natural environment is a great concern for many of us. Populations of native species are in decline, ecosystems are being polluted and biodiversity is decreasing. Living here all my life I see this and it matters to me enormously.

**What I will work towards**

If elected I will:

- Promote habitat restoration and protect the outstanding natural beauty of the Surrey Hills and North Downs for future generations
- Create and maintain nature corridors so that wildlife can thrive across our landscapes
- Ensure we have the right people and resources in place to manage our land for all of us
- Push for planning decisions that put the protection of our green spaces first

I am proud to call this place home and I will work hard to protect everything that makes it special.

In the upcoming local elections, I believe we have a real chance to change the way our new West Surrey Unitary council is run. Too many opportunities for our communities and the people in them have been squandered by short-term thinking. I want to start making decisions that think bigger and longer term and build resilience to the challenges we're all facing together. That's why I'm standing for the Green Party.

**Roads and potholes**

On the doorstep, the state of our roads comes up time and again. Potholes are a real problem in our area and I believe that's largely down to expensive third-party contractors using poor quality materials. If elected I will:

- Bring roadworks teams in-house
- Make sure potholes are repaired to a proper standard

**Our natural environment**

The state of our natural environment is something I feel strongly about. Native species are in decline, our ecosystems are being polluted and biodiversity is falling. This matters to me and I know it matters to many of you too. If elected I will:

- Promote habitat restoration
- Create specific nature corridors
- Make sure we have the right people in place to manage our green spaces properly

We come from many walks of life with different life experiences and different political views, but we can all unite in our desire to keep our community strong and good to live in. We may vote for national parties in national elections, but in local government the most important thing is to have capable representatives who live locally, like Gren and myself who are both long term residents of Egham, who know the new ward well, and care about its future.

Egham town has elected Independent Resident councillors over many years. We are your neighbours; we live here and know the place and the local issues well. We’ll work with other councillors from any political party, for whatever brings most benefit to the new Egham ward and we’ll vote independently, unwhipped, however we think best for our community. Egham is simply a better place to live for having strong, local, Independent representation. Our concern is to safeguard the new Egham ward’s interests in the future, when there’ll be fewer representatives and decisions will be made more remotely, by people who may not know Egham at all.

Individual details are below, but if elected, our shared aims will be to:
* Foster a collaborative approach, like Isabel has done as part of the Runnymede Council Alliance at RBC. This will be essential if, as likely, no single party gets overall majority.
* Maintain services to support people living active lives, independently within their homes, in spite of budgetary pressures on the new authority.
* Keep strong engagement with the River Thames Scheme to maximise benefits for those in high flood-risk areas in Egham Hythe and Egham.
* Oppose gravel extraction at Milton Park and Whitehall Farms.
* Fight for improved pedestrian access to Runnymede meadows.
* Embed high standards for SEND at the new council.
* Press the new authority to take potholes seriously.
* Support the Local Cycling and Walking Infrastructure Plan, giving more choice on how to get places, reducing congestion and bei

Hi

My name is Danni and I’m standing as the Green Party candidate for Egham. 💚

I’ve got over 100 years of family history in Egham but also went to Strodes College and RHUL so I understand better than most how to balance the conflicting needs of residents and students.

I spent 5 years working as a family support worker here in Runnymede — so there’s a good chance I’ve crossed paths with many of you already in that role helping to navigate EHCPs, housing, finances or life after separation or domestic abuse.

Prior to this I have worked as an SEN teacher to children with social emotional and mental health needs, focussing on students with EBSNA.

I have run intervention projects in partnership with YOT and social services using judo as well as Princes Trust “TEAM” program.

I have also worked as a job coach across the new “West Surrey” helping people overcome barriers to work such as poor health, caring responsibilities and criminal records.

I now live “on the other side of the tracks”, which means I get the daily joy of the railway crossing — so I promise I’m very grounded in the realities of local life! 🚂😤

I care about keeping Egham a great place for families — and I believe in a bit more hope, a bit less hate, and actually listening to people.

Thank you for trusting in me.

Kind Regards

Danni

Cai grew up as a young carer for his disabled father with MS, and he now works full-time representing care providers working for a national charity. Passionate about finding ways to help with the kind of issues that councillors helped his family with, he wants to be able to give something back to the community.

Rahul has worked in the public sector and corporate world and knows how to get things done - tackle complex problems. He got into politics because, as an Egham resident, he is passionate about delivering for the local community - whether it’s the railway downtimes, fixing potholes, fighting litter picking or taking up local campaigns for parking spaces, street lights or better facilities for our children and local businesses.

I have lived in Egham for 31 years. I was a manager at British Airways. Now retired, I am a Member Nominated Trustee Director of its Airways Pension Scheme (APS), run an allotment at Boshers in Egham, am a member of the Egham United Services Club (often serving beer at its festivals) and have recently become a COVID-19 volunteer vaccinator. I want Surrey and Runnymede to have a better representative mix of political parties and believe a Liberal Democrat council presence, when given the chance, has generally improved the efficiency and service of local government in the UK.

With many years of experience, Marisa understands local government at every level and how to get things done. Moving to a unitary authority is going to be hugely challenging and needs experience and a safe pair of hands which Marisa can provide. She has a strong track record of delivery having won millions of pounds for Surrey and getting issues prioritised in her current Englefield Green seat. She has been on the Cabinet leading the Environment portfolio for many years and Surrey has been recognised for its leadership on protecting nature, the countryside and tackling flooding and protecting the Green Belt. As a councillor she holds monthly councillor drop-ins for residents and works closely with local residents associations. On the council she has worked to ensure resident voices are heard on controversial planning applications, and has chaired the climate change board and the standards committee.

Local issues she focuses on include the Gosden Hill development and addressing flooding issues through the local flood forum.

I'm standing as a candidate for the Conservative party.  I think it is important to make the distinction between national issues and the local issues in the election.  There are many things that we would like to change at a national level, but at a local level we are primarily dealing with services.
I will commit to working with my fellow councillors in the first term to:
- provide the first hour of parking on council sites in Guildford free with an hourly rate afterwards to help more people support our local town businesses;
- resurface the worst 24% of all roads in West Surrey, not patch potholes poorly;
- bring the garden waste bins within the normal refuse collection system, saving people money; and
- working to retain more of the money you pay in council tax within Surrey.
I'm a chartered accountant who has spent his career working in large multinational businesses and I would use my experience in the council particularly on financial matters.

Adam is 26 years old and a lifelong resident of Burpham. Raised and educated in the local area, Adam lives in Weylea Farm with his family.

Having worked for Guildford's former MP Angela Richardson for 3 years, Adam already has a grasp of the issues that are affecting Burpham and Merrow, and will work diligently alongside residents, local community groups and other councillors to achieve the best outcomes for the community.

I grew up in Haslemere, went to school in Guildford and have lived here most of my twenties. I'm a veterinary nurse in specialised referral practice in Guildford. My local Green Party provides a community where we come together to push against the divisive rhetoric of the far right.

**What I will fight for**

If elected I will:

- Fight for our planet's protection
- Stand up for women's rights, abortion rights and LGBTQIA+ and trans rights
- Challenge the blatant sexism entwined in everyday life
- Refuse the idea that refugees and immigrants are unwelcome. They are being exploited and used as distractions from those spewing hate instead of hope and community

I am a young adult who lives and works in Guildford and I want to represent this community I call home. I love that Surrey is a place where you can work at the cutting edge of the games industry and still be five minutes from nature. Working in tech here has shown me that we don't have to choose between moving forward and protecting the natural beauty that makes our home special.

Community isn't just a word to me, it's about the work we do together. I love helping the next generation find their feet in their careers and bringing neighbours together through sport to build a stronger sense of belonging.

The Green Party shares those values and I think that matters right now more than ever.

**What I will work towards**

If elected I will:

- Work to deliver more social housing with sustainable features and developments that genuinely work for the communities they are built in
- Make sure consultations with local people are properly heard and that developers stick to their agreements
- Push to hold water companies and sewage works to account. The River Wey has seen sewage dumps nearly one hundred times lasting over one thousand hours. That cannot continue
- Monitor our waterways and fight for clean rivers for everyone in Surrey

Surrey has given me a home and a career I love. I’m committed to protecting everything that makes it special.

I started my career in live events in Guildford 17 years ago, working in the arts spaces our town is proud to cultivate. Living the mindset of: real life doesn’t pause and reset, you fix problems now while everything is moving.

My young family is here. We should feel proud of and safe in Guildford, yet too many people see a managed decline while families struggle.

Residents face sky-high rents in uninhabitable mouldy homes that make children sick. 
Schools unable to provide enough wraparound care, SEND provision harder to access. 
Our river is dangerously polluted, sewage levels far beyond safe limits, and a stench that infects our homes.

Greens in the room make a difference by acting on solutions:
Bringing schools back into local authority support to improve SEND and wraparound care.
Clamping down on rogue landlords renting unsafe mould-ridden homes.
Introducing stricter local oversight and enforcement to stop sewage dumping.

It’s time to end stagnation caused by a tactical vote that doesnt exist here anymore and reverse managed decline.

A life-long Guildford/Surrey resident, Marco has been serving the community since growing up cooking in his local family restaurant, before going into creative design and promoting Guildford’s entertainment industries, continuing to encourage local business within his wife’s millinery studio and keeping his toes firmly in the community working also as a postman.

When he’s not pounding pavements he loves nothing more than treading water, with a passion for aquatics and love for Surrey’s waterways. Marco has been a proud protector of marine life and certified as a open-water lifeguard, is naturally heartbroken by the pollution Thames Water has been allowed to flood into our rivers and streams; he could not be more dedicated to doing everything in his power to bring the Wey and its navigations back to their natural beauty, along with the thriving wildlife, social life and business opportunities that it woul regenerate.

I am standing because I believe the Green Party stands for something more than the politics of the day. It refuses to cling to a status quo that is leaving our communities behind and wants to build a stronger foundation from which something new can grow, bringing us together rather than dividing us, whatever our differences.

I am a researcher at the University of Surrey and a resident here in Guildford South East. Outside of work I am a keen choral singer at Holy Trinity Guildford and you will find me most weekends either out hiking or playing tabletop games.

**What I will work towards**

Two issues concern me most for our area. If elected I will:

- Work to protect our community from the ridiculous levels of debt West Surrey will inherit. Too many opportunities for local people have been squandered by short-term thinking and I want to help change that
- Push to hold water companies and sewage works to account for the pollution of our waterways. The River Wey has seen sewage dumps nearly one hundred times lasting over one thousand hours. That cannot continue
- Seek to deliver more social housing with sustainable features and developments that genuinely work for the communities they are built in
- Make sure consultations with local people are properly heard and that developers stick to their agreements

I care deeply about this area and want to do my part in helping it become the best it can be for everyone who lives here.

A long-term ward resident, I’m dismayed by the level of misinformation, petty party
politics and lack of accountability from some councillors. Did you know Guildford’s
handing the new Unitary a deficit budget (£6M by 2028/9), that it’s sitting on £7M of
developers’ funding for education, or that its external auditors have declined to sign 
off on its accounts for the last 5 years, and recently confirmed that the uncertainty in
how much money it has (or doesn’t) had increased further over the past year; are you
frustrated you cannot contact your councillor?

I’m standing to listen to residents and businesses, to help inform and influence the
debate and, if elected, collaborate with councillors from all parties to deliver the
infrastructure and services we expect. This year, when day-to-day control remains
with Guildford and Surrey, and the role of new unitary councillors is primarily to set
up the new council I’d shape the Unitary to best meet our community’s needs,
retaining Guildford’s unique identity.

PRIOTITIES From talking with residents, my priorities include:
•	Potholes: I’d reallocate budgets (eg  the further £300k Guildford has set aside for planning a Guildford Parish) for immediate fixes and ensure a long-term maintenance schedule.
•	Housing and development: I’d end the scandal of unoccupied homes, and support a new town centre vision repurposing unwanted retail/office space to housing and community areas, including medical and youth facilities, whilst safeguarding the more rural areas of the ward such as the Chantries. I’d support direct discussion with landlords and retailers, eg to help open the old House of Fraser store, I’d also review council procedures to ensure they are best supporting the third of residents who live in private rented accommodation (the highest proportion in the area).
•	Crime and safety: The tragedy of January’s fatal stabbing was one facet of a ward that suffers from the highest levels of anti-social behaviour and violent crime

I am standing as a Lib Dem candidate (Guildford South West division) in the Surrey County Council elections.

My priorities include:

* helping residents and businesses recover from the pandemic, including more help for mental health, reduce the need for food poverty / food insecurity.
* working with residents, Guildford Borough Council and businesses to make Guildford a cleaner / greener place to live and work eg improve air quality, better infrastructure for electric vehicles, reduce car idling);
* improve road safety through ‘safer neighbourhood schemes / school streets’, 20mph roads where people walk, cycle or scoot; create ‘joined up cycle routes; and increase the usage of our local bus services; fewer potholes and more resurfaced roads!

I live with my family in the heart of Guildford South West. Now semi-retired I had previously and for many years ran an IT consultancy business.
I was elected in 2023 and was duly selected for a number of committees. On the Planning Committee I will actively defend our green spaces. On several occasions I have made a stand to defend the green belt and won.

I sit on the Overview & Scrutiny, and the Audit & Risk Committees which scrutinizes the daily running of the Council to ensure checks and balances are maintained and finances are closely controlled.

I am also a former soldier. I have served my country. Each year you will see me in Guildford High Street in the lead up to Remembrance Sunday shaking a tin. As Armed Forces Champion I advocate on behalf of the families of Service personnel. I also have the honour of being Honorary President of Sqn 261 Air Cadets.

With the challenges ahead and the debt we will inherit, if elected I will continue to work with and support the team in its endeavours for a balanced budget.
I will continue to stand up for maintaining our green spaces.
I will carry on to support military families to ensure that they are treated fairly in the wider community

I am a local resident who cares deeply about wellbeing, education, and community resilience. My background is in engineering and education, which means I cut through complexity, make evidence-based decisions and get things done. I hold decision-makers to account and push for outcomes that are fair, transparent and built to last.
I volunteer regularly at Guildford’s Repair Cafe and I will fight to protect our green spaces for future generations.

Key Policy Priorities:
	•	Protecting Green Spaces. I will stand up for Guildford’s parks, countryside and local environment and resist development that threatens them.
	•	Education & Young People. I will push hard to make sure every child gets the high-quality education and support they deserve.
	•	Community Resilience. I will bring people together to build stronger, better-connected neighbourhoods that look after their own.
	•	Transparent Decision Making. I will demand evidence-based decisions and call out anything that is unfair or unaccountable.
	•	Wellbeing. I will put the long-term health and wellbeing of Guildford residents at the heart of every decision.

I’m seeking selection for Guildford South West because I believe the Green Party can offer something increasingly important locally: credible, values-led politics delivered with care, and consistency.
I’ve lived in this part of Surrey for the past ten years and know it as a place of strong communities alongside real pressures. Families here are navigating stretched public services, rising living costs, and growing anxiety about the future. Too often, politics feels distant from these everyday realities. I want to help change that by building trust through visible, practical, and reliable local representation.
For much of my adult life, formal political activity wasn’t an option. While serving in the RAF, political neutrality was expected. Even after leaving service, it took time to find a political home that aligned with both my values and my belief in evidence-based, constructive public service. As the Green Party has grown in credibility, professionalism, and local impact, that alignment has become real. I’m standing now because I believe I can contribute meaningfully.
Professionally, I work in complex organisations delivering large-scale change. That means balancing competing priorities, working constructively with people who hold different views, and turning principles into outcomes that actually land. I bring a calm, analytical approach, strong organisational skills, and a deep respect for accountability. As a candidate, that translates into disciplined campaigning, clear communication, and a willingness to do the unglamorous work consistently.
My primary policy focus is education, particularly Special Educational Needs provision. This is not abstract for me. I am the step-parent of two teenage children who have been through the SEN system, including the tribunal process. I have seen first-hand how complex, adversarial, and exhausting the system can be. It is painful, expensive, and deeply unfair for families who lack the time, resources, or specialist knowledg

Following the resignation of Cllr Fiona Davidson,  Reform-UK Guildford is proud to announce our candidate, Dale Layman – a Guildford resident for over 30 years with a long and successful career in pharmaceutical sales management. Dale combines professional competence with deep local roots, and he will be a strong voice for the people of Guildford South East.

Reform-UK Guildford’s position is clear: national issues now shape local politics. Surrey County Council has signed binding Net Zero commitments and is pushing ahead with unitary authority reforms that will control transport, housing, climate and public safety.

These are not “local tweaks” — they are national agendas imposed locally and residents feel the impact every day through ever-rising council tax, anti-car measures and wasteful spending. The new unitary authorities will only tighten this grip, affecting how we travel, where homes are built, how businesses operate and how services are delivered.

That is why Reform-UK provides a clear alternative. We are the only party prepared to challenge Net Zero dogma, fight anti-car policies, and cut through bureaucratic waste — standing up for residents where it matters most.

It’s time for a new voice in Guildford — one that restores transparency, accountability and common sense to local government.
Vote Reform-UK’s Dale Layman in the Guildford South East by-election on 16th October

I’m standing because Guildford deserves better, better decisions, better priorities, and a council that puts residents first. As a research scientist, I’m used to working through complex detail and making evidence-based decisions, and I’d bring that approach to the council.
The biggest issues I hear on the doorstep are housing and protecting local services from West Surrey debt caused by LDs and Conservatives. We’ve got an eight-year wait for social housing, so we need to bring empty homes back into use and deliver genuinely affordable homes that reduce that list. At the same time, we must protect frontline services by being forensic with spending, cutting waste, not what people rely on.
And yes, I give my word: Green councillors aren’t bound to a party line, we’re there to represent our communities. I will always put residents’ interests first.

I grew up and was educated in Surrey, where I now run an independent bookshop in Guildford town centre with my partner, Hannah. I see the largest challenges facing Guildford West as the housing crisis and the debt the new unitary council will inherit from neighbouring councils. If I am elected I will try to fill the vacant council housing in Guildford West, and fight for the residents when it comes to taking on debt that wasn’t originally theirs. Above all, I will always listen to residents and put their needs first.

candidates.p: The Peace Party - Non-violence, Justice, Environment
candidates.stmt:
I would like to be the next MP for Guildford to show our constituents that they CAN live in a land where Peace is achieved only through non-violence, that Peace is the product of Happiness and Wellbeing. A manifesto encompassing these Qualities means, for example, everyone having an adequate income, enjoying healthy life, top quality education and where there is trust in a well-functioning government.

John is a retired teacher and administrator in education.

He is Chair of the Peace Research and Education Trust.  He holds various offices within the Religious Society of Friends in Guildford and area.

Oliver Leach: Proven Service, A Local Voice for Haslemere

​Dear Neighbour,

​Haslemere is more than just where I live—it is the community I serve every day. As your candidate for the West Surrey Unitary Council, I am running to ensure that our town’s unique needs are at the forefront of every decision. My platform is simple: Haslemere First.

​A Record of Listening and Action

​I believe a councillor should be someone who truly understands the heartbeat of their town. My experience in Haslemere is rooted in local action and genuine service:

​Local Business, Local Heart: As the owner of Oliver’s, I see the challenges our high street and our residents face firsthand. My shop is a hub for conversation, and I am proud to support our community by providing free hot meals once a month at the Haslemere Food Bank. I don’t just talk about community spirit; I live it. ​The Power of Listening: Since 2011, I have served as a Guildford Samaritan, including three years as Branch Director (2016–2019). This role taught me that the most important skill a representative can have is the ability to listen—really listen—to people. Listening is my strength, and it is exactly what I will bring to the council. ​A Champion for West Surrey: I will bring that same dedication to the Council. Whether it is advocating for better local infrastructure, protecting our green spaces, or supporting our local families, I will be a calm, determined voice for you. ​My Promises to You

​If elected, I will use my experience as a business owner and a volunteer to:

​Be Your Voice: I am trained to listen and equipped to lead. I will be your direct line to the Unitary Council, ensuring Haslemere’s concerns are heard loud and clear. ​Support the Vulnerable: Using my background in service to ensure that community support and local welfare remain a priority in our region. ​Protect Our Town’s Character: Ensuring that our growth is sustainable and that we preserve what makes Haslemere a special place to live. ​P

Terry is standing for election to Waverley Borough Council and Haslemere Town Council this May.

Originally from South Africa, where for many years he was an anti-apartheid campaigner, Terry is now a British citizen and has lived in the UK since 2003 (and in Haslemere since 2006).

Following a career in market research and later in education, Terry is now semi-retired, and works part-time delivering medicines for a local pharmacy, which has given him an acute awareness of the difficulties faced by some of the elderly and vulnerable people in our community. As a long-term rare cancer patient, he is a trustee for GISTCancerUK, and also a consumer representative with the NCRI Sarcoma Clinical Unit.

Terry has been a Haslemere town councillor since 2019, serving on the Climate and Biodiversity Emergency, Finance and Governance, and Planning committees - since 2022 as chair of the latter.

I am standing to be the Conservative Party Councillor for Haslemere on the new West Surrey Unitary Authority.

My family have called Haslemere 'home' for generations, and I want to give back to the town that has done so much for us by serving residents in Haslemere and the surrounding villages. As someone with business experience, I will bring economic competence and sound judgement to the council, and will be a visible and active presence across Haslemere.

I am Operations Director at a tech startup in London that I've helped build for the last 8 years, and a listening volunteer at Samaritans in Guildford. I am also the proud owner of a Countdown teapot, having won 8 games in a row on Channel 4's Countdown in 2024 (granting me the title of 'Octochamp'!).

Feel free to get in touch with me and my fellow candidate Graham Betts at grahamandtoby4westsurrey@gmail.com. And make sure to vote on May the 7th!

I’ve been a Haslemere resident and community advocate for since 2008, focusing on practical, hands-on solutions for our town. In 2019 I was elected as a Haslemere Town Councillor, and re-elected in 2023. 
With a background in youth and family support, I understand the everyday challenges people face. As your local councillor, I don’t just talk about change; I work on the ground—whether auditing our green spaces or protecting our local environment. I am committed to making Haslemere a fairer, more supportive place where every resident, from young families to seniors, can thrive.

STATEMENT FROM 2024 General Election: I am delighted to be standing as the Green Party candidate for Farnham and Bordon. I've been a town councillor in my home town of Haslemere since 2019 and welcome this opportunity to represent The Green Party. A Haslemere resident for over 20 years, I previously lived in Whitehill where my daughter attended Woodlea Primary School and where I was Committee Secretary of Bordon's Alexandra Preschool, my son's nursery. My background is in family support and Early Years Education and I worked for 10 years at the Tennyson's Sure Start Children's Centre in Haslemere until 2019 when the centre was closed. The withdrawal of this valuable support service, alongside numerous other government cuts, was one of the galvanising forces that drove me to get involved in local politics. I want to see more Government-funded services that make a real difference to the lives of residents of all ages. I am passionate about community projects that improve life for both residents and our environment. Having been involved in setting up and running local covid response initiatives -- such as haslemerehelp and the Haslemere Community Store - I also lead the establishment of the Water Rangers Water Quality Testing Haslemere Hub, aiming to take action and make a discernible difference. I would like to see the country move towards a cleaner, greener, more sustainable way of life and c

It has been of  great concern to observe the decline of the UK under previous and current governments, having abandoned British citizens by failing to deliver on their promises and to carry out the will of the people.

Some of the concerns that are affecting British citizens on a national and local level are:
* open borders and agenda to eliminate sovereign nations
* lawlessness and injustice in the judicial system
* loss of freedom of speech and religious expression (most notably Christian), loss 
  of parental authority, breakdown in the family unit.
* an absence of Christian principles which has underpinned the UK and other free 
 societies for centuries.
* decline in merit-based advancement, high taxation and government overreach.
