These are just a few of the globalist/Marxist/secular humanist policies, along with social immorality which have contributed the the UK's decline--spiritually, economically, morally and militarily.

As a Christian, I believe in the return to long-held Christian values, which are pivotal in achieving liberty, prosperity and justice for all people.

I believe the Reformation of this country must begin at the local, grassroots level and this is why I am standing for West Surrey Council representing Haslemere.

These issues require boldness to promote truth, love for nation and to put the best interests of UK citizens and taxpayers first. REFORM UK is the only party with the bold, decisive leadership necessary to deliver this change in direction.

I will:
* Promote the preservation of Greenbelt land, local character, British culture and patriotism.
* Repair roads and update utilities in a timely manner--minimising disruption to local traffic.
* Challenge wasteful policies, releasing mon

I have lived in Haslemere since 2013 and became a Town Councillor in 2023. With a background in science I worked in IT for many years. Since Covid, like so many I had to find a new path. Not having to commute has been an opportunity for me to refocus on what I value most, to connect more with the local community and work for the environment.

Haslemere is a wonderful place - we are lucky to live amongst beautiful countryside, and the town and surrounding villages boast a community of people who are active and engaged. I’ve been involved in many local initiatives to improve the area and am a strong advocate for equitable housing and better transport.

Transport
Most of us both live near and use roads, so getting transport right matters. Potholes are a serious hazard and could be better addressed by bringing repairs back in-house to improve quality. Lower speed limits make roads safer and more pleasant with little impact on journey times, studies show they actually improve traffic flow.

Town Centres and High Streets 
People spend more time (and money) in pleasant, pedestrian-friendly town centres. Therefore encouraging active travel and improving public spaces improves our quality of life and supports local businesses.

Energy
We live in a volatile world—it’s common sense that as a community we work to become more resilient and independent of oil and gas, by insulating homes and making sustainable energy sources (like solar panels) a priority.

Education
Improving education starts with listening to parents’ and teachers' real concerns with empathy and respect. By fighting for the resources needed to support special educational needs (SEND), we can build a system that works for every child—and ensure no one is left without future prospects. This would benefit all children including those who do not need extra support.

Housing
Housing must be both affordable and sustainable for all. That means pushing for genuinely affordable homes, built to high environmental standar

I have lived in West Surrey for decades and have worked locally for 15+ years as a law firm business owner and solicitor. I hope to now use my dispute resolution, legal  and risk management skills in service of my community.

I am passionate about mitigating climate change. The Greens have a range of carefully thought through policies to tackle this locally.

I am also keen we address the problems of housing and homelessness. Again we have a variety of policies on this

Catherine Young has lived in the Horsley’s for more than 25 years, working as a Teaching Assistant at The Raleigh whilst bringing up her son and becoming more involved in her local community by joining West Horsley Parish Council. Catherine was elected with a landslide majority as your local councillor back in 2019, representing Clandon & Horsley. She has held many positions on GBC Boards and working groups and is currently Chair of the Licensing Committee, Vice-Chair of Overview & Scrutiny, R4GV’s representative on the Local Plan Panel, covers the Climate Change Board when needed and represents GBC on the Surrey Hills National Landscape Board.
Catherine is one of our candidates for the election to the West Surrey Unitary Authority on May 7th 2026.  She will be standing for the Horsley Ward which is made up of East & West Horsley, Effingham, Ockham, Ripley and the village of Wisley.

Catherine says “I am proud to have supported many residents over the years and am excited to be standing for election once more. The opportunity to serve as a local councillor has been incredibly frustrating at times, but every achievement, no matter how small, renews my determination and motivation to carry on.”

She is also very involved locally as Chair of Horsley’s Climate Action Network group which she set up, and a founding member of Horsley’s Repair Café. She finds driving for the Wheel of Care incredibly rewarding and is a member of the local WI (although attending these meetings is a challenge!).  Catherine is a member of St. Mary’s Church in West Horsley, and if she has any spare time she can be found gardening at home or on her allotment (which she shares with her partner).

Dear Neighbour,

Our villages to the east of Guildford are very special places. But they are changing, and we need to protect what makes them unique places to live and work.

I want to bring some fresh thinking and practical problem solving into our communities to get things done for residents. I am new to local politics—not a failed politician of the past—and I am focused on bringing energy and determination to make a real difference for local people. Whether that means holding developers to account and protecting our green spaces, tackling illegal encampments, getting potholes filled, or backing our local businesses, I will stand up for residents every time.

I am proud to represent a Conservative Party in renewal, both locally and nationally. It has returned to the values we share of family, responsibility and enterprise, and I believe it is the only credible answer to the harm being done to our communities and our country. The Lib Dems do not have those solutions, Reform cannot win here, and R4GV are a spent force. Only the Conservatives will deliver the practical solutions we need in the Horsleys and across Surrey.

I am campaigning every day for a fresh and stronger voice for local people, and I hope that you will support me on Thursday 7th May.

Sam Tough

Dennis Booth and his wife Mary have lived in the Guildford area for more than 40 years and raised their children here. They have always loved being active in their community, assisting (and representing) local residents: they were founding members of a residents’ association and are actively involved in their local Church.”

Dennis is one of our candidates for the election to the West Surrey Unitary Authority on May 7th 2026. He will be standing for the Horsley Ward which is made up of East & West Horsley, Effingham, Ockham, Ripley and the village of Wisley.

Dennis says “I’m proud to be standing for election as an independent, resident-focused voice for our villages. As a former Borough Councillor and Mayor of Guildford, and now your Surrey County Councillor, I have seen clearly how those in power so often ignore what we residents want, riding rough-shod over our needs, views and wishes. This was never more apparent than the flawed decision to grant planning permission for Wisley New Town.”

“As a resident representing residents, I will take our battles direct to the Unitary Authority. I’m not interested in the backbiting, point-scoring and wider agenda of national political parties; I’m focused on what we need and want for our local people and the place in which they live”.

I’ve lived here with my family for over two decades and care deeply about our community. Volunteering at Cobham Area Foodbank during Covid showed me how much a strong, connected community matters when things get tough. I have organised community events, run small businesses, supported local projects, and I remain hopeful for the future of our area.

I am standing because I want to see my community unite around common sense policies for the benefit of all the people who are a part of it.

One of the things I love most about this area is its natural beauty and I want to make sure it stays that way for future generations. That means making the right decisions now and not leaving our problems for someone else to deal with later.

**What I will work towards**

If elected I will:

- Work to ensure our roads are properly maintained. Potholes are a real problem in our area because of expensive third-party contractors using poor quality materials. I will push to bring roadworks teams in-house and ensure repairs are carried out to a proper standard
- Work to protect local assets from being sold into private hands
- Seek to introduce community municipal investment so local people can invest in their own area to fill our cash reserves and fund community projects
- Work to address the significant debt we will inherit in the new West Surrey Unitary Authority.

I love this area and I want to play my part in making it the best it can be for everyone who lives here.

Ann-Marie Barker has lived locally with her husband David for almost 30 years and has represented Goldsworth Park for nine years. Since 2022, she has led Woking Council, helping shift it from risky property ventures back to delivering core local services. Locally, Ann-Marie supports community meetings, litter picks and biodiversity projects, and has secured new facilities around Goldsworth Park lake. Professionally, she works in research with major businesses across manufacturing, rail and property.

Steve Greentree has lived in Knaphill since 1999 and locally for over 30 years. After a successful international career in biotechnology and medical research, he was elected as a Knaphill councillor in 2023 and joined the council’s executive leadership team in 2024. Steve has helped transfer community centres into local hands and secured funding to protect green spaces. A proud grandparent of six, he remains committed to standing up for residents and holding decision-makers to account.

"Maureen and her husband Steve have lived in Shepperton for the past 12 years. Having taken early retirement from a career in local government. Maureen believed she could put her experience in Housing and Adult & Children’s Social Care to good use within the community. She was elected to the Council in May 2015, joining the borough’s Cabinet the same year. She holds responsibility for Community Wellbeing which includes the borough’s services for older people and children. Maureen has enjoyed working with local residents, supporting and representing them to resolve matters they have sought her assistance with, as well as her wider council responsibilities.

She is also a governor at Ashford and St Peters Hospital."

"Sinead is a local resident, having lived in the Borough of Spelthorne since 2004. She is married with 2 children, both of whom have received excellent educations from schools in the Borough. Sinead is an interim Housing Professional, previously working as a Head of Housing Services for a housing charity. Prior to this Sinead spent 16 years working in the housing departments at Hounslow and Hillingdon Councils.

A local activist for many years, Sinead was the chairman of a local youth charity, Staines and Egham Sea Cadets and committee member for Ashford North Residents Association. She is also an active supporter of the local campaign to include Staines railway station in the zone 6 pricing scheme.

Sinead has played a leading role in a number of local projects and community improvement programmes. Sinead has developed a keen knowledge of local issues, having served 5 years as a committee member for a residents Association.

Sinead has been a Spelthorne Borough Councillor since May 2015 and a County Councillor since May 2017. Sinead is the cabinet member for Adult Social Care and Health at Surrey County Council.

“I feel very proud and privileged to represent the residents of Staines at Surrey County Council, and I hope to be elected to in the Borough elections in May to continue with my good work at Borough level. I work very hard to preserve and improve the character of Staines.’’"

My name is Jason Gelver. I was born and raised in Whitton, Twickenham, before moving to Sunbury-on-Thames in the mid-80s. Since then, I’ve lived in various parts of Spelthorne, developing strong connections across the community.
I’m a dedicated family man, happily married with two children. My youngest is in his final year of secondary school, diligently preparing for his exams, while my eldest is a trained medic and a part-time firefighter—both embodying a strong sense of responsibility and service. Our household wouldn’t be complete without our two dogs, who relish long walks in our beautiful local parks.
Politically, I once aligned with the Conservative Party, but over time, I’ve grown disillusioned with their leadership and lackluster performance. When Labour took office in 1997, I was deeply concerned, seeing it as the beginning of a slow and painful decline for our country. UKIP briefly brought a glimmer of hope, but breaking into the political establishment proved challenging. Then came Reform UK—a party that resonated with my values and those of many like-minded individuals around me. Their fresh, dynamic approach inspired me, and before long, I became actively involved, signing up as a prospective candidate.
Beyond politics, I juggle multiple responsibilities. I run two businesses, support my wife’s entrepreneurial ventures, and oversee an IT department for a military sub-contractor. With over 30 years in the IT industry, I’ve progressed from a helpdesk operative to an IT Service Delivery Manager, a role that has taken me across the globe—including time spent in an active war zone supporting our military.
I consider myself a ‘jack of all trades’—someone unafraid to roll up my sleeves and tackle challenges head-on. This hands-on approach, combined with my wealth of practical experience and sound judgment, makes me a strong candidate for election.
While I may be new to politics, I see this as an advantage. I have no entrenched political baggage or hidden agen

"Amar Brar has lived in Stanwell for 39 years now.

Has been married for nearly 50 years and has 3 grown up children

Running her own local company as a Financial Adviser for 33 years now

Previously worked for British Airways in Computer operations

Her interest as a councillor is improving roads, transportation and housing."

Steve has been a Borough Councillor for the last four years, and Co-Leader of Runnymede Borough Council for the last two. He lives in Egham with his wife, daughter, dog and cat, and is a professional photographer.
Steve sees the imminent changes to local government across Surrey as an opportunity to do things better. But there are also significant challenges ahead.
"This is no time for beginners! West Surrey Council will be a new organisation where we will be effectively re-inventing the wheel. The learning curve for a new councillor is steep, even in an existing Council with other, more experienced Councillors and Officers to support and guide you. But not only will WSC Councillors need to get on-board with the increased casework - 2 Councillors will be replacing the 7 Councillors you currently have - but they will be setting up an entirely new council structure at the same time."
"With regard to Laleham & Shepperton, I think the big ticket item must be delivery of the River Thames Scheme flood defences. But more directly, and what many people are talking about, I would want tighter licencing around HMOs as well as greater transparency in local planning applications so residents can see what's being proposed in their areas."
"Furthermore, I would push for pothole maintenance and similar maintenance contracts to be brought in-house at the earliest opportunity. The Council spend a fortune paying external contractors and the standard of work is generally appalling."

Matt is 25 years old and has lived in Spelthorne all his life. His formative years were spent in attendance at Thamesmead Secondary School and 1st Charlton Air & Sea scouts, where he fostered a deep love for the local area and all it has to offer - particularly our wonderful riverside! He is keen to bring a fresh perspective to the council and represent the younger voices in our vibrant community.

Harbouring a deep love for animals & wildlife, Matt has found fulfilment in volunteering for various animal welfare organisations such as the RSPCA, Battersea Dogs & Cats home, and other wildlife organisations that control invasive species, helping our native biodiversity to thrive. Alongside this, Matt believes in the power of a strong sense of community and spends time cleaning up the borough with our brilliant Spelthorne Litter Pickers.

Currently working as a Transport Planner, Matt has a unique skillset that lends itself to ensuring the successful delivery of critical council services. This includes fostering proactive relationships with council officers to achieve tangible results, reviewing policy documents (including local plans) to ensure proposals are practical and effective, and understanding how strategic planning impacts communities at a local level.

Matt is described as someone who is eager to get things done, with an acute attention for detail and a distaste for wasteful behaviour. He is keen to work with residents of Laleham & Shepperton to enhance the area and advocate for better youth services, protection of our green spaces, sufficient transport infrastructure, and proper maintenance of our roads and public spaces.

Michael has lived in Spelthorne for 12 years. He has a deep love the area, citing its unique open spaces, riversides, local shops and cafes as well as the myriad of local organisations the work to strengthen the community. Alongside this, he notes that the borough is home to many warm and friendly people whom he meets on a daily basis. Michael is aware of and empathetic to a broad range of issues that are being experienced across our community. He strongly believes that the impending Council reorganisation, which will see Spelthorne council replaced by a West Surrey & South Middlesex authority, is a critical opportunity to ensure that a quality team of representatives, free from the status quo, are in place to tackle these issues head on.

Michael has a PhD in Chemistry and, before retirement, worked in the consumer goods industry at an international capacity. He strongly believes that the skills he practiced whilst managing multi-discipline global teams and regulatory agencies would be an asset to the role of Councillor, particularly as part of a shadow council setting up the new autority. His key areas of concern are the increasing levels of crime, the state of local roads, polluted waterways, the lack of flood defence, protecting our green spaces and reducing the concerning level of debt we are to inherit from day one. Michael believes that the Liberal Democrats are the only party who can tackle this mammoth task effectively.

In his personal life Michael is a carer for an elderly person with complex health needs and, like many in the same position, is concerned with the lack of support for carers and those with health issues across our community. During his free time, he volunteers as a steward at Brooklands Museum, helps to run a Music Society in Twickenham, coaches a Ladies Hockey team and is an annual volunteer for the London Jazz Festival. He says “I recognize that the fundamental duty of a Councillor is to listen to residents needs and, if elected, this for

Richard has lived in Lower Sunbury for almost forty years and has a deep understanding of the issues facing the ward. A few years ago, Richard retired as the managing director of his own business which employed 27 staff. Richard's company exported to America and the European Union, so he knows from personal experience the impact of trade barriers, including tariffs, on British businesses. Richard has also led a campaign against cold-calling, and has been heavily involved in the Spelthorne Design Code consultations.

Paul has lived in Lower Sunbury for 16 years. Having worked in education for 30 years, including as Headmaster of a school in central London, Paul now works establishing British curriculum schools overseas. As a family man, Paul understands the value and importance of schools and youth organisations to parents and their children in Lower Sunbury and Halliford. He has for many years benefitted from what the local community has to offer and now he is committed to giving back to that same community by ensuring that the new unitary authority works for the people of Lower Sunbury and Halliford and that our distinct community and identity are not lost to the new authority.

Before moving to Halliford last year, Buddhi was a hardworking community activist and a prominent local campaigner in Twickenham. He initiated a project to establish a trade association to help local business community & residents in Heathfield-Twickenham. Buddhi is a big supporter of local businesses.

He was a governor of a local school and has also worked as volunteer teacher at LBV Sunday School of Chiswick. Buddhi was also a trustee of several charities including Whitton Community Association.

Buddhi will engage with residents on issues relating to environment, education, local businesses and residents’ safety. Buddhi will also campaign on behalf of  residents on  concerns with regard to roads, pavements and overgrown trees and will closely work with County Councillors to sort out issues.

Buddhi works for International Trading firm as Business Development Director . He is married with three young children.

I’m Adrian Barbrooke, and I’m standing for The Green Party in the Shalford ward of West Surrey because I believe local politics should deliver practical results for the people it serves.

My background is in fast-paced, delivery-focused environments, where I’ve been responsible for turning plans into outcomes. I want to bring that same approach to local government—making it more transparent, more accountable, and focused on long-term value rather than short-term fixes.

A key priority for me is expanding access to green energy. I support increasing grants for low-income households so we can cut carbon emissions while also reducing energy bills for those who need it most. The transition to a greener future must be fair and inclusive.

I care deeply about community cohesion—supporting local initiatives, strengthening neighbourhood connections, and making sure everyone in Shalford feels heard and represented.

I’m also campaigning to bring Surrey’s highways maintenance back in-house. I believe this will improve accountability, deliver better value for money, and result in better-maintained roads for residents.

To support younger people, I want to extend eligibility for the Surrey Link bus pass to those aged 30 and under—helping more people access work, education, and their communities.

I’m standing to deliver practical change, put residents first, and help build a greener, fairer future for Shalford.

I'm standing as the Green Party candidate in the Shalford ward of West Surrey because I believe that effective local politics can transform the civic and natural environments we all rely on for a good life.

I'm sixty-six and married with twin daughters. I have been based in Hurtmore for twenty five years. My working life has involved careers in teaching, journalism and international relations, running large teams and commercial operations. I am now retired and a trustee of the Guildford Book Festival, committed to enriching the cultural life of our community.

My professional experience focused on developing and delivering good strategies and making the right choices to win. As your representative in local government I will ensure the Council makes the right choices for you delivering the high quality services you and your community demand and deserve.

**What I will fight for**

If elected I will:

- Deliver low cost affordable housing in the right places
- Secure a fair deal for carers
- Ensure high quality education for every child whatever their ability in well run and well resourced schools
- Make affordable and reliable public transport a reality that gets you where you want to go
- Protect and restore our natural environment

The Green Party is the only party that can be trusted to aim high. Let's aim high together.

I’m a portfolio educator and community connector, passionate helping people turn ideas into action.

With over 25 years in education, my work now brings together facilitation, community engagement and creative thinking. I run local events, support community projects and create spaces where people can connect, reflect and move forward with confidence.

My approach is simple: listen well, bring energy, and help people find their voice.

As a local Guildford resident, a local business owner, and a motorbike owner, I understand the frustrations of my neighbours, other business owners and the drivers, and riders, that have to swerve left and right just to avoid potholes. I hope I'm given a chance to help put the new Unitary Authority on the right track with good money management, sensible decisions and always with residents in mind.

I believe the Green Party stands for something more than the politics of the day and that is why I want to represent you. It refuses to cling to a status quo that is leaving our communities behind and wants to build a stronger foundation from which something new can grow, bringing us together rather than dividing us, whatever our differences.

I have lived in the Shere area for twenty-three years and am actively involved in sporting and cultural life here. I am a member and past volunteer with the Surrey Wildlife Trust, an organisation close to my heart. I worked in healthcare from 1983 until my retirement, ending my career at the Maudsley Hospital in London. Outside of that I am a keen gardener and grow my own fruit and vegetables.

Living where I do, I see close up the damage that poor farming practices and pesticide use are doing to our countryside and I feel strongly about it. The pollution of our waterways by privatised water companies is equally unacceptable to me.

**What I will work towards**

If elected I will:

- Push to hold water companies and sewage works to account. The River Wey has seen sewage dumps nearly one hundred times lasting over one thousand hours. That cannot go on
- Work to restrict pesticide use and challenge farming practices that damage our natural environment
- Seek to deliver more social housing with sustainable features and developments that genuinely work for the communities they are built in
- Make sure consultations with local people are properly heard and that developers stick to their agreements

I am proud to call this place home and I will work hard to protect everything that makes it special.

I am standing because I want to see my community unite around common sense policies for the benefit of all the people who are a part of it.

**What I will work towards**

If elected I will:

- Work to ensure our roads are properly maintained. Potholes are a real problem in our area because of expensive third-party contractors using poor quality materials. I will push to bring roadworks teams in-house and ensure repairs are carried out to a proper standard
- Work to protect local assets from being sold into private hands as we face the significant debt we will inherit from the way the boundaries of the new West Surrey Unitary have been drawn up
- Seek to introduce community municipal investment so local people can invest in their own area to fill our cash reserves and fund community projects

I am here and I am ready to work hard for you and this community.

As the Labour & Co-operative candidate for Shere, I represent shared values. I am a British European, raised across Surrey—from South London to Woking, then Guildford.

Diagnosed at seven with Dyslexia and Dyscalculia, I’ve developed a forward-thinking approach to solving problems. Special Educational Needs support deeply matters to me.

In recent years, our Council services have struggled to meet today’s challenges: caring for the natural world, fostering self-reliant neighbourhoods, engaging differing views, strengthening our roads, ensuring clean waterways are free from privatised waste, and providing a healthy standard of living that doesn’t cost us our wages or the Earth.

Poor Conservative decisions and divisive politics should not define our future. Broader representation on West Surrey Council can realise our community values, together we can make real progress. I pledge to serve Shere and work closely with all six hardworking Parish Councils. Yesterday’s problems should not shape tomorrow’s opportunities.

My name is Laura Barker and I’m standing as the Liberal Democrat candidate in the Staines by-election on the 16th of October.

Staines is my home: I have lived here for over a decade. There is little I enjoy more than walking my dog Penny by the river. However, it saddens me to see the polluted state of the water, which has occurred as a result of corporate greed. Cleaning up our rivers is one of the Liberal Democrats’ top priorities.

I love Staines. Sadly, empty units on the high street and anti-social behaviour are making it less attractive. Many people in our town don’t feel safe, especially women. This is why the Lib Dems want to invest more money into community policing. Despite the best efforts of fantastic community organisations like the Spelthorne Litter Pickers, littering also continues to be a significant problem in Staines. When an area looks run down and neglected, crime only escalates.

Having seen what my husband, Cllr. Harry Boparai, has achieved as a local councillor I would also like the opportunity to improve the lives of local residents. As a mother to three children who attend local state schools, I know the challenges young people and parents face in our area. Conservative cuts to youth services, to our libraries, community hubs and youth centres, have decimated opportunities for young people and damaged the social fabric of our nation.

This is why I am privileged to work for an organisation which uses the power of sport to transform the lives of young people. I have seen first hand the importance of positive role models and, if elected, I will push for Spelthorne Borough Council to do more in this area.

Climate change poses the greatest threat to the future of our planet. I am appalled at the complacency of the Conservative and Labour governments in tackling the climate change emergency. The Lib Dems form the largest group in the current administration at Spelthorne Council, but we do not yet have an outright majority.

The more Lib Dem cou

Chris is an experienced councillor and is currently the deputy leader of Spelthorne Council, he is well known for his deep commitment to residents. Chris has lived in Staines for decades and understands the issues our town faces. He has worked hard to fix many of them.

Staines is being slowly eroded by left wing policies, enabled by a coalition of labour, liberals and independents.  I cannot stand by and residents are urging me to stand against this.  The Council owes millions of pounds and has been severely criticised by the government inspectors for they way the residents have been failed.  
residents are not fools.  They know that the coalition of chaos at Spelthorne has sat on its hands for the past 5 years and wants to blame everyone but themselves.

I was the Mayor in 2023 and what a tremendous honour.  I met thousands of our wonderful residents.  I want to help to make things right for them.  Good luck to all!

I have lived and worked in Staines for about 40 years, have been a Borough councillor for the Staines ward since 2023 and was also a councillor between 2015-2019.

My main areas of focus are:

- Stopping the overdevelopment of Staines
- Making Staines a better place to live, work, shop and relax
- Curbing antisocial behaviour
- Ensuring Staines has a strong voice in the new, bigger authority
- Tackling the borough’s debt
- Improving access to the riverfront and making Staines more pedestrian-friendly
- Putting residents first, not politics

I have played an instrumental role in saving the former Debenhams building from demolition (see the "Save Staines High Street From Greedy Developers" Facebook group), creating a sound Local Plan, a Design Code that takes groundwater flood risk into account, reducing debt and a plan to improve Staines.

I am an accredited independent coach, consultant and contemporary performance producer, with a strong background in festival development, community building and inclusivity in the arts. These skills enable me to bring people together from different backgrounds to work together to create memorable and democratic experiences and changes.

I joined the Greens in 2026 having been let down by years of Labour abandoning socialist principles and drifting towards the kind of right wing policies I abhor.

I am passionate about improving access to inventive performance of all kinds, and I spend my working life generating exciting new ideas with artists, and helping people and organisations live and work at their best. I see cultural flourishing as a key part of challenging the environmental crises we face.

Born in Ashford, raised in Sunbury, I worked for years in Staines, including my first job at 16 running the (not-at-all-rigged-I-promise) games at Thorpe Park! If you fell off the wobbly ladder or won a giant teddy there circa 2001, chances are I was involved.

I love this part of the world and will work hard to support our local communities with a strong Green perspective.

candidates.p: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party
candidates.stmt:
Why am I here? What are my policies? Why should you vote for me?
These are all good questions. They are left as an exercise for the reader.

Let's think about you, the voter reading this. You're the sort of person who looks at these election nerd websites in an attempt to find out more about the people you're going to be voting for. You know I'm standing as a Monster Raving Loony; you can probably figure the rest out.

**My main campaign promises:**
1. Build a great big glorious iron curtain between West Surrey and East Surrey, to protect us from the inferior East (Wesside is de best!)
2. West Surrey will immediately launch a surprise pre-emptive strike against East Surrey. West Surrey must take the first napkin, and assert geopolitical dominance.
3. Rename "Staines-upon-Thames" back to "Staines".
4. More funding for our libraries. They do a good job.
5. Tackle the council's debt by gambling it all away at Admiral Casino on the high street.
6. Ensure that any lingering debts from Spelthorne Council are safely offloaded onto the West Surrey budget, and then enact a surprise referendum on Middlesexit. We will re-establish Middlesex!
7. Rename "Staines" back to "Staines-upon-Thames".
8. Promote more car usage (free parking in all car parks, unpedestrianize Staines High Street, more roads, fewer pavements etc) in order to solve our pothole problem (we do not have enough potholes, we need to increase the rate at which we erode the roads so we can get even more of them (potholes will continue until morale improves)).
9. Save taxpayer money on expensive proprietary software licenses by migrating the council's administrative backend computer systems to TempleOS.
10. Rename "Staines-upon-Thames" back to "Ad Pontes".

There will be more common-sense uncontroversial policies as well. You have been warned.

So, what are you waiting for? Go and register to vote (and pester everyone you know to register to vote) otherwise I'll be the only person who shows up to vote and I'll end

As a young adult from Staines, a recent graduate and now working for the NHS, Harleen recognises the unique challenges faced by residents of all generations. She is standing to be a dedicated advocate for Staines South and Ashford West, to prove that our voices matter, our future is exciting and that we all feel empowered, excited and genuinely connected to our community's future. Harleen will fight for opportunities and support to turn our shared concerns into tangible action for our local area.

Cara has lived in Ashford since 1998 and has strong roots in the local community. She is an experienced psychotherapist and personal tutor, and holds an Honorary Teaching Fellowship with Birkbeck, bringing a deep understanding of listening, advocacy, and responsible decision-making. Cara is the local Neighbourhood Watch Co-ordinator and works closely with residents to improve community safety and support neighbours.  Her children have attended local schools, giving her a strong, practical understanding of the issues facing local families.
Philip is a local resident, having lived in the Borogh of Spelthorne since 1979. My wife and I were married at St Matthews church. Both of our children were educated schools in the borough.

As a local campaigner for over two-decades and a resident I understand (**our**) issues, in **Stanwell Moor, Stanwell & Ashford North** as I live here with you. As a **Local Champion** and previously as a **Councillor** it has been a privilege to serve our community. I am not a career politician, I’m a local man who is dedicated to improve our area, who understands how Government works and who knows - **How to get things done!**

As a community activist I’m well-known for standing up for local residents, so when so many residents asked me to return to politics I decided to stand. I am also standing because Surrey County Council and Spelthorne Borough Councils are in a financial mess due to incompetence and gross mismanagement and this cannot be allowed to happen in the new West Surrey and (hopefully) South Middlesex Council.

WHAT PEOPLE MIGHT NOT KNOW?   I have previous Council experience, I know how they work, how to get the best from them and how to make changes from within a Council - And GOD knows this new Council, WILL NEED to CHANGE!

I also have business and town planning experience and have previously been responsible for the management of the largest Council property portfolio in the UK. So I would ask residents to trust my experience to reform issues that have been so neglected by current administrations.

I’m standing as this election is a transformative **political moment** for West Surrey as the new authority will merge **6 local councils** to oversee the delivery of services that affect the economic, social and environmental wellbeing of **600,000** residents into one 'mega' council.

I’m standing as this **Labour government** is ruining our country, the **Conservatives** have allowed Surrey County Council to become one of the worst managed authorities in England and **Liberal Democrat** incompetence has seen the once proud **Spelthorne Council** decline into special measures and facing an unprecedented debt crisis.

With Spelthorne holding just **14
Harry is a local campaigner and has lived in spelthorne his whole life having been born at Ashford hospital (Middlesex) and raised in Stanwell.

I have lived in Spelthorne with my family for over a decade. My two children attend local state schools and I also work in the borough. For 30 years my career in arts and education has been primarily spent providing practical support and organising and campaigning for changes that improve people’s lives. This has encompassed working with the NHS to improve access to injury treatment services to keep people active through sport and dance and working with parents and young people to ensure their educational needs are met. I have served as a nursery school governor and on the local residents’ association setting up a community garden and successfully lobbying the council for improvements to the estate on which we lived. I am a natural problem solver and solution finder and realise that some of the frustrations felt in the local community may be solvable on a local level whereas others may need robust representation and communication with and through our local MP. If elected I pledge to listen, communicate honestly and with care, and act where I can on behalf of the local community to create a safer, healthier environment for us all to live in.

Gerald has lived in the local area for over forty years and personally experiences the issues affecting his neighbours. Gerald says that “Stanwell and Stanwell Moor have many issues that seem to have been either ignored or unsatisfactorily dealt with for so long. A fresh approach is needed.”

Malgorzata has practical experience of leadership and responsibility. She currently serves as Chair of Trustees for a Polish school, where she oversees governance, works with families, and helps ensure the school is run transparently and effectively. As a first-time candidate, she wants to bring this hands-on experience into local government and be a focused, reliable voice for residents.

“Since being elected as your county councillor in 2022 by only 15 votes, it has been my honour and privilege to serve the residents of Sunbury Common and Ashford Common for the last 4 years.

“I was joined by Suraj as a borough councillor in 2023 and we have worked together very effectively as a team to address long-standing issues, strengthen neglected infrastructure and to support the many community groups in our area.

“Issues addressed include flooding on Beechwood Avenue, racing in the Tesco car park, fighting against HMOs and solving persistent fly-tipping in Kinross Drive/Close. We have also secured funding for local youth organisations including the girl guides and scouts, as well as St Saviour’s and Ashford Methodist Churches, Spelthorne Sports and Dunford Hall.

“Our canvassing confirms that this election is a straight fight between the Liberal Democrats and Reform. We are determined to stop Reform’s divisive politics taking hold in our community.

“Sunbury and Ashford Common will be represented on the new unitary authority by two councillors.

“Labour and the Greens cannot win here. The Greens cannot even find a second candidate. However, a vote for Labour or the Greens could let Reform win.

“Therefore, please cast BOTH your votes for Suraj and me.”

Rory was born in Sligo, Eire to Anglo-Irish parents, and settled in the UK in the late 1970's. Growing up in South Harrow, NW London in a loving single-parent household with two older siblings. Rory has lived in Sunbury (on the edge of the Spelthorne Constituency), with his wife Sandra and two children, since 2007.

Rory first got involved in politics in 2019, as part of the grass-roots campaign team, for the Brexit Party in Spelthorne. However, it was the draconian measures of Covid 19 lockdowns and dangerous group-think within Westminster, that spurred him into direct action.

Firstly, for Sunbury Common & Ashford Common in the 2021 Surrey County Council elections, with a 4% share of votes. Subsequently, for Sunbury Common in the 2022 by-election for Spelthorne Council, with a 7% share of votes. In the 2023 Local Election for Sunbury Common, with a further increase to 11% share of votes. And more recently secured 17% of vote share, in the 2024 General Election.

Caring and passionate, Rory has embraced campaigning for the reforms so desperately needed on a national level and for a return to the values of 'small government', providing over-sight whilst promoting free expression, freedom of movement, freedom of choice, and free-market principles.

Rory has been instrumental as Chairman, in the launch and running of the Reform UK Branch in Spelthorne. Bringing together a talented Executive Team and motivated Candidate's to stand in this Mays important Local elections.

I am a software engineer by profession and I have lived in Catherine Drive, Sunbury Common for many years, together with my wife and a toddler. I joined the Liberal Democrats because i believe in establishing an inclusive community where local opinions are fairly represented at the decision-making level. I actively support local campaigns to save the last remaining green spaces in Sunbury Common now that the Conservative Government has removed protection for the green belt. If elected my priorities will be supporting those who are most in need, tackling anti-social behavior and preserving green spaces.

Time for Change. Time for Action.

A proud local resident rom last 15 years. Dr Anil Khedkar is focused on what matters most: safer streets, better roads, support for local businesses, and protecting our green spaces with more opportunities for young people.

With a practical, forward-thinking approach, Dr Khedkar will work to deliver real results and build a stronger, cleaner, and more connected community for everyone.

Real Results, Not Promises

"Naz Islam, a local Ashford family man with 4 children. Naz moved into Spelthorne in 1996 soon after leaving college.

As a life long conservative, Naz joined the party at the age of 17 and has been actively involved ever since. His passion for active involvement led Naz to stand for conservatives in May 2015, resulting in becoming the borough councillor for Ashford Town ward. Naz also won the election to represents the Ashford division of Surrey county council on 4th May 2017 with a huge majority.

Naz is seeking to gain good economic development of Ashford, where Ashford thrives through proportional development and believes in fighting for quality and affordable housing for our younger generation.

As a family man Naz cares with passion for local community issues such as expansion of schools to support places for future generations, believes in further GP surgery to support the loss of Thames doc, is anti green belt development, and support transport issues such as Oyster card, zone 6, oppose bus service cuts, along with creation of local job opportunities for next generation.

Naz is always ready for challenge, and caring for the uk’s future. Always takes time to listen and discuss both local and national issues."

I have lived in Woking for over 20 years, and teach at a local secondary school. I am an experienced local councillor with a track record of improving our community and supporting residents with issues that matter to them. As an experienced teacher and school leader, I also want to give Children’s Services and SEND provision the shake-up and improvement it badly needs, after decades of underfunding and poor management under the Tories.

Raised locally, Ian Richardson studied politics at the University of St Andrews and law at Trinity College Dublin. During the pandemic he worked in retail before moving into political campaigning. Living in West Byfleet, Ian is committed to working for local residents and improving education, particularly for neurodiverse students. Motivated by his community volunteering during COVID, he brings energy, empathy and determination to representing Byfleet and West Byfleet, both on and off the rugby field

Neil Willetts: Independent Candidate for West Byfleet

I am standing as an independent to focus on what matters most to our community not party politics.

West Byfleet faces two urgent issues:

Our roads are already under strain and getting worse
The West Hall development threatens to overwhelm our villages

This development, in its current form, is unsustainable. It adds hundreds of homes without proper provision for roads, schools, healthcare, or policing.

I will:

Oppose the West Hall development in its current form
Push for real improvements to local roads and traffic
Demand infrastructure comes before development

I am independent, which means I answer only to residents not a party. I was asked by Amanda Boote Mayor of Woking to stand and to use my skills for the betterment of our community.

If you want clear thinking and strong representation on the issues that matter locally, I would welcome your support. I actually live in West Byfleet and have done since 2001

Sean was born in Wimbledon and raised in Lancaster, Sean now lives in Woking with his family. His political beliefs are rooted in a lifelong commitment to UK sovereignty and opposition to the ever-growing burden of government regulation. A former GB Under-21 water polo player, Scout leader, and international touring musician, Sean brings determination, leadership, and real-world experience to Reform UK. After becoming disillusioned with the failures of the traditional parties, Sean joined Reform in 2024 and is now a passionate advocate for real change.

I am proud to be standing as one of the Conservative candidates for Thorpe, Longcross & Ottershaw in the upcoming West Surrey Council election.

This area has a strong sense of community and deserves councillors who listen carefully, work hard and stay focused on the issues that matter most to residents. Having worked locally since January 2013 and been a local resident since 2017, I understand many of the day to day concerns people raise about our area.

I am an energetic campaigner and community minded local representative who believes in practical action, visible local leadership and making sure residents are properly heard.

My priorities include standing up for residents affected by the A320 scheme, supporting safer roads, tackling crime and anti social behaviour, protecting green spaces, and opposing gravel extraction that would put extra pressure on local roads and the environment.

I want to be approachable, hardworking and focused on getting practical results for Thorpe, Longcross and Ottershaw.

I have been a borough councillor for Longcross, Lyne & Chertsey South since 2019. This ward is now part of the West Surrey ward of Thorpe, Longcross & Ottershaw which also includes Lyne, Chertsey South and part of Virginia Water.

Since May 2024 I have been one of the Co-leaders of Runnymede Borough Council, when the Runnymede Council Alliance was formed bringing together councillors from Thorpe, Egham and Englefield Green Independents, Labour and the Green Party. Our achievements include:
Reduction of the Runnymede Council debt by £30m

Wed communicate all year round, not just at election time, holding monthly surgeries and always responding to resident communications in a timely manner.

I was elected as a Borough councillor in 2019 for the ward of  Longcross, Lyne & Chertsey South which is part of the new ward of Thorpe, Longcross & Ottershaw.

I have a wealth of business and local authority experience and keen to carry on representing the local community.  Since May 2024 I have been part of the Runnymede Council Alliance who collectively manage the council. Our achievements include:

Reduction of Runnymede Borough Council debt by £30m
Production of a balanced budget
Additional protection for nature and its recovery
Reduction of Runnymede's Carbon footprint

Upgraded car parks
Employed a Social Inclusion Officer to assist those in financial need
Introduced the Big Green Week
Invested in Local Communities using Developer Contributions
Invested in NHS capacity for Runnymede
Work as a team with other councillors for the benefit of the community
Regularly communicate with all residents in our ward (Liberal Democrats)
Hold monthly surgeries for all to attend (Liberal Democrats)

Hello everyone - more about me…

💼 By profession, I trained as a Chartered Surveyor, which gives me a technical background to support residents across a wide range of issues.

• Chair Waverley Council’s scrutiny committee
• Bramley Parish Council Chair

📨 In my day job, I work for Sir Jeremy Hunt, so I am very well acquainted with tackling local issues right across our area - from roads and infrastructure to planning and public services.

⚖️ I have also been proud to co-lead the battle for justice for residents unfairly hit by huge householder CIL bills, standing up for homeowners simply wanted to improve their homes and were caught unfairly by legislation.

⛽️Bramley Fuel Leak caused huge issues for residents across  wider community and I was proud to lead the response to it on behalf of my community.

💧 I’ve also spent a huge amount of time battling Thames Water on sewage capacity, flooding resilience and infrastructure failings, pushing for real investment and practical solutions for communities.

💙 USING MY PASSIONS TO DELIVER FOR OUR AREA

🏘️ Sorting out our chaotic planning system so development happens in the right place — with roads, schools, GP capacity and other infrastructure actually delivered alongside it

💧 Holding Thames Water and other utilities to account when local infrastructure fails

🌿 Improving everyday life for everyone across our villages

I work relentlessly hard for the communities I represent, and I care deeply about protecting and improving the place we all call home.

Alongside my excellent co-candidate Cllr Ke

He started his police career in 1980, working as a beat officer at Egham. Over 30 years Kevin rose through the ranks to Chief Superintendent in charge of policing the West  Surrey division (the boroughs of Guildford and Waverley), retiring as a Detective Chief Superintendent investigating serious crimes.

Following his retirement, Kevin became a parish councillor at Alfold, and in 2015 was elected as the Borough Councillor for Alfold, Cranleigh Rural and Ellen’s Green. He has been a member of the Executive Council, holding portfolio responsibilities for Community Safety, Health and Well-being, and operational planning and enforcement.

In his spare time Kevin enjoys watching local sport. He is an active member of Alfold Football Club, watching them whenever possible. He has been the chairman of the Alfold Sports and Social club for the past 10 years, a community hub which provides a variety of social and sporting  events throughout the year.

I feel very lucky to live in such a beautiful village with a kind and compassionate community, but there are things that need fixing and I would relish helping with that.

I have already been working hard for our villages for the past couple of years, getting potholes fixed and graffiti cleared, campaigning to end the disgusting amount of sewage being poured into our streams (you may have seen my petition to Southern Water) and supporting the fight against Conservative-approved plans to drill for fossil fuels in our beautiful countryside.

We need an active, visible and hardworking local councillor, that will fight for our villages and get things done for residents.

When half of Chiddingfold is without water, or when Combe Lane is closed unexpectedly for weeks, or when the bus doesn’t turn up day after day, it should be our local councillor that’s demanding action and keeping residents informed on what’s going on.

Paul (right) is a keen member of the local community, helping with both youth football and cricket teams (at Albury FC and Wonersh & Shamley Green JCC).

He is currently Chair of the Waverley Lib Dems and is committed to improving community services for all as well as preserving our areas of outstanding beauty.

I am standing for the West Surrey ward of Waverley Eastern Villages because I believe in politics working for the local community and I want to play my part in making that happen.

I have been a Bramley resident since 2004 and this community matters deeply to me. My working life has taken me from teaching English in Spain to twenty years in the NHS where I served as a Union representative, then into IT management and later into nature conservation. I am a regular volunteer with a variety of local charities and when I am not doing that you will find me playing the ukulele.

That background in conservation means the state of our natural environment is something I feel very strongly about. Native species are in decline, our waterways are being polluted and biodiversity is falling. The River Wey has seen sewage dumps nearly one hundred times lasting over one thousand hours. That is unacceptable and water companies must be held to account.

**What I will work towards**

If elected I will:

- Push to hold water companies and sewage works to account for the pollution of our rivers and waterways
- Work to deliver more social housing with sustainable features and developments that genuinely work for the communities they are built in
- Make sure consultations with local people are properly heard and that developers stick to their agreements
- Draw on my NHS and union experience to ensure our community gets the public services it deserves

I am proud to call Bramley home and I will work hard to protect everything that makes it so unique.

Mark settled in Waverley with his family 15 years ago to continue a business career that had taken them to live and work around the world. He has been a passionate local community volunteer, charity trustee and school governor.

Over the last four years, Mark (right) has been the Waverley administration’s Finance portfolio-holder and, with substantial private sector business experience, he believes in basic financial competence. The Lib Dem-led council has balanced the budgets and maintained services with no cuts (and even supplemented them) despite unprecedented pressures. Mark will continue to speak out for areas like ours outside of Farnham’s town centre to be treated fairly.

These elections in May are for local government, but Farnham Town Council has wrestled with the impacts of wider issues like Brexit, Covid and inflation while maintaining, even expanding, its local events and facilities. It’s frustrating that the Council doesn’t have primary powers over most infrastructure, but we hold those who do to account.

Mark will continue speaking out against inappropriate development, and for the basics like road repairs and better from the Farnham Infrastructure Project. He work with all Councillors for Badshot Lea and Moor Park’s residents.

If elected on 7 May 2026 as an Independent, I’ll be guided by the following principles:
- Keep the council out of party politics.
- Protect our rural way of life.
- Help residents with their problems

My immediate priorities, based on what residents have told me most concerns them, are:
- Protecting local interests during the momentous changes to local government. In particular, fighting to minimise Woking Council’s debts being unloaded on to local residents.
- Campaigning to save our wonderful countryside and villages from reckless over-development.
- Curbing anti-social behaviour - fly-tipping, excess noise, litter, petty crime.
- Getting on top of pothole repairs!
- Opposing the expansion of Farnborough Airport - our skies are too noisy as it is.
- Opposing gimmicky projects that sound good  but waste money without providing real help to people.

Ged was educated in North Wales and went on to read Economics at the University of Greenwich in London and has lived with his wife and three sons in Hindhead for over 20 years. Ged is a former Deputy Leader of Waverley Borough Council and former President of Haslemere Community Rugby Club.

From a modest background of being a builder’s son, Ged is accustomed to hard work and delivery. Ged's political views are centre-right believing in low taxation, supporting business and entrepreneurship, whilst ensuring public services are fit for purpose and deliver value for money.

Ged has been self-employed for over ten years, working as a consultant for banks and finance houses in London and the Home Counties. Ged helped set up the Haslemere Youth Hub to improve services for children and young people in his home town and now sits as a trustee on the charity board.

Ged’s decision to stand for Reform is based on a belief that a new, voter-focused approach is needed and that that the main political parties have deserted their founding principles.  Ged’s key priorities are Jobs, Education and Healthcare.

I am standing for Waverley Western Villages because I care deeply about this community and I have already had the privilege of serving it. I was first elected to represent my neighbours in Binscombe in a by-election in 2021 and that experience has only strengthened my commitment to fighting for the people who live here.

I grew up in South Africa during the transition from Apartheid to democracy under Nelson Mandela and attended one of the country's first non-racial schools. That experience shaped my belief in social justice, democratic accountability and the idea that politics should genuinely serve people. I came to the UK for what was supposed to be a gap year and somehow never left. My husband and I moved to Binscombe in 2019 and it quickly became home. I currently teach in a secondary school in Woking and see every day the pressures that families are facing.

The state of our natural environment is a great concern for many of us and something I feel strongly about. Native species are in decline, ecosystems are being polluted and biodiversity is decreasing. I also see firsthand in my work the impact of underfunded schools and the struggle families face when the right support for their children is not there.

**What I will work towards**

If elected I will:

- Promote habitat restoration and create nature corridors to protect the biodiversity of our local area
- Ensure we have the right people and resources in place to manage our green spaces for everyone
- Make sure SEND provisions in our schools are properly funded so that no family has to fight for the support their child needs
- Help our communities grow closer together by supporting local initiatives for young people including sports, arts and culture
- Give greater support to our key workers and push to improve access to sustainable energy for all

This is a beautiful part of the world and I will dedicate myself to keeping it that way.

Hello, my name is **Simon Lightman**, and I am standing as a **Green Party candidate** because I believe local politics should be rooted in care, honesty and long-term responsibility.

I want to help build a council that listens properly to local people, protects the places we share, and makes decisions with future generations in mind. Too often, communities feel that things are done _to_ them rather than _with_ them. Whether the issue is housing, roads, public services, sewage, green spaces or support for young people, local people deserve a stronger voice in shaping the future of their area.

My professional life has been built around education, sustainability and helping young people become active citizens. I have worked with schools, charities and community organisations to support youth leadership, environmental action and more meaningful participation in decision-making. I believe those same principles matter in local government. We need practical, joined-up solutions that improve everyday life now while also preparing our communities for the challenges ahead.

**If elected, I would work for greener, fairer and more accountable local government.** That means:
- Protecting nature and improving community infrastructure
- Supporting families and young people
- Pushing for better environmental standards
- Making sure public money is used ethically and responsibly

For me, standing for the Green Party is _not just about the environment._ It is about building communities where people feel heard, where decisions are made transparently, and where the future is treated as something **we are responsible for together.**

Attia Aslam has lived in Surrey for over 20 years and is a secondary school teacher with a lifelong commitment to education and community wellbeing. A Woking Borough Councillor, she also works with charities and serves as an outreach officer for the Surrey Coalition of Disabled People.

Attia founded the Angelic Network and leads health, inclusion and digital access projects locally. She is passionate about equality, supporting vulnerable residents and protecting local services for future generations.

Zafar Rasab has lived in Woking North since 1987 and attended local schools. Through running a general store and estate agency, he has helped generations of residents settle into the area and supported many community initiatives. Actively involved in local projects, Zafar is committed to strengthening community ties, supporting young people and promoting sustainable living. He wants to ensure Woking North remains a welcoming, connected and forward-looking place for families and local businesses.

Tahir Aziz lives and works in Woking, where he runs his own mortgage advice and property management company. He is an experienced councillor, having worked for residents for seven years and Leader of the Labour Group in Woking Borough Council. As a member of the Planning Committee, he has consistently stood up for the rights of residents, campaigning for affordable homes and against overdevelopment of the town centre. He has also proposed reform of Woking Borough Council’s debt collection practices, campaigning for reduced use of bailiffs.

Cllr Anila Javaid is Labour’s candidate for Woking North and a Borough Councillor for Canalside. She is Chairman of Woking Labour and has spent over 20 years building and leading 7tnine Developments, a UK-based consultancy delivering business innovation, transformation, and performance improvement across the UK and internationally.
Anila brings a practical, evidence-led approach to public service: clear priorities, budget discipline, and a focus on outcomes residents can actually feel. Since being elected in 2023, she has supported hundreds of resident cases, working with residents, officers, and community partners to resolve problems and improve local services.
Her priorities are rooted in a simple principle: a fair deal for Woking. That means stronger negotiation on behalf of residents, better scrutiny of proposals, and transparent decision-making — particularly in areas where contracts and delivery arrangements shape everyday life, including waste and street services, housing management, regeneration programmes, and the IT systems behind access to support.
As Woking moves into a new unitary authority, Anila believes it is more important than ever to have representatives who will show up, ask the hard questions, and insist on measurable performance from delivery partners. Her commitment is to listen, act, and deliver — with integrity, accountability, and public money treated as precious.
Voice of you

Ian Johnson has over 30 years’ experience serving Woking as an elected councillor, executive member and portfolio holder for housing. He has played a key role in creating Woking Foodbank, supporting homeless services, improving housing management and securing free children’s burials at Brookwood Cemetery. Ian brings deep local knowledge and a strong record of standing up for residents, particularly those most in need, and is committed to shaping effective services in the new council.

Louise Morales was first elected to Woking Council in 2011 and Surrey County Council in 2025. She has served as Mayor, Chair of Planning and now Portfolio Holder for Planning Policy and sits on the Surrey County Council and Woking Borough Council planning Committees.
A self-employed gardener, Louise brings a strong environmental focus to her work. Louise is a tireless advocate for residents, tackling issues from fly-tipping to social care and service delivery. She is committed to improving planning, sustainability and active travel, and to shaping fair, future-focused policies for the new West Surrey Council.

Harrison is a local resident, teacher and business owner with a lifelong passion for conservation. He has said:

“Woking needs a fresh start, led by people with an uncompromising commitment to the area. We face a severe fiscal crisis, which the amalgamated West Surrey unitary authority will inherit... We can improve our general financial situation by innovating and modernising how things are run. Here are some of the ways a Green council might look to accomplish this:
1) Use Community Municipal Investment to fund local projects.
2) Bring highways maintenance in-house, instead of paying contractors to do quick patch jobs on potholes.
3) Invest in local, person-centred social care, focusing on prevention and early intervention.
4) Take SEND access seriously at the outset, so that money goes to helping children, instead of to fighting and losing lawsuits."

Candidate web page: https://wokinggreens.org/harrison-getz-one-of-our-2026-candidate-for-woking-south/

* Helping disabled people and their families with issues like SEND schools and housing.

Peter Graves has served as a Pyrford councillor for four years and was a member of the council executive with responsibility for waste management and green infrastructure.
