Before entering local politics, Peter worked in a local secondary school. He is committed to improving everyday services and residents’ quality of life.
Peter is part of a great team for Woking South East, along with Ellen Nicholson.

Ellen Nicholson is a Liberal Democrat candidate for the Woking South East Division to the new West Surrey Unitary Council on the 7th May 2026. A local Woking councillor since 2021, Ellen is a strong, local and hardworking campaigner and has a proven track record of delivery for residents; on renegotiating contracts that deliver improved provision in community services and leisure, and she recognises both the opportunities and challenges in establishing the new West Surrey Council.

Ellen is a successful NHS professional who lives locally, who uses her experience of getting things done from her professional expertise, as a councillor, a local hospital governor and former director of a SME, to deliver on the issues that matter most to residents.

"I love Surrey. I’m proud to live here. Our towns and villages are special places, In order to deliver a successful new West Surrey Council, residents in Woking South East deserve a strong independent-minded Councillor with a good track record of delivering effective services and putting local people first.

My name is Ben Edward Maynard and I am standing to represent the Conservatives in Woking South East in the new West Surrey Unitary area.

I lived in Pyrford for 38 years and attended Pyrford First and Middle school and currently run my Domiciliary care agency in the area, a job i love, caring for the elderly every day.

I'm passionate about Woking south east and it would be a privilege to be their representative. Fixing the pot holes and protecting the green belt are just two of my top priorities. I would also be visible, interacting and trying to help the community all year round and not just in the run up to elections.

Woking-born Leslie Rice is proud of his hometown and works closely with residents on planning and Green Belt issues, pushing for environmentally responsible, community-led decisions. A Chartered Management Accountant, Leslie has held senior leadership roles with UNICEF and international banking groups. His strong background in financial and risk management gives him the skills to scrutinise council operations effectively, ensuring high standards of transparency, accountability and ethical decision-making for local residents.

Dale Roberts is Deputy Leader and Portfolio Holder for Finance, helping steer Woking through financial recovery and modernisation. With over twenty years of private-sector leadership experience, he brings a calm, honest and detail-focused approach to complex challenges. Dale believes residents deserve clear communication, practical solutions and reliable services. Standing for the new unitary authority, he will continue working to build a stable, transparent council that manages money responsibly and puts people first.

I moved to the UK from Poland with my family almost a decade ago and I couldn't have asked for a better home than Woking. From a teen that couldn't speak English, I am now a writer, tutor, volunteer, event organiser and law student - including administrative law. 
This country has provided me with opportunities I couldn't even dream of anywhere else.  It's out of gratitude that I want to give back to the community that gave so much to me. That is why I am a conservative - I belive in respecting tradition, pragmatic and level-headed decision making. 
I do not want to shift blame on others for the past mistakes made in Woking. The Conservatives have apologised and are moving forward, whilst others remain stuck in the past. We want fresh voices, new ideas and optimism. 
In just under a year, we have new unitary councils being created. We cannot allow past problems to carry over. I am standing to be the voice of debate, public engagement and opposition this Council so desperately needs. It's time to reset. It's time for common sense to enter the room.

I am standing as the Labour and Co-operative candidate for Pyrford because I believe the Liberal Democrat and Conservative Councillors, distracted by the huge debt they have created, are failing to listen to the residents and businesses of Pyrford. As they publicly squabble over who was responsible, their failure to come up with a plan and build for a better future, risks holding back our Towns and Villages. As a Labour Party member, I have long campaigned for a fairer future for all. As a member of Labour’s sister party, the Co-operative party, I believe in empowering local communities and building better connected networks across our community. My background as a Self-Employed engineer working on large broadcast events has not only taught me how to work as part of a team but also how to lead such a team and how a motivated group of people is a powerful instrument of co-operation. If elected I pledge to be the voice of Pyford’s residents, to work with our existing Labour Councillors to maintain the Council’s focus on our communities, to push for sustainable, green policies to be at the heart of every Council decision and to make sure that the borough has a realistic and competent plan to deal with the debt crises while building a more solid foundation for our future.

I’d like to give the residents of Woking a choice at this election.

The government has given us record taxation, debt and relies on immigration rather than training up the next generation.

All the globalist Westminster parties are pushing us down the road of conflict in Eastern Europe.

I am standing for the Heritage Party as I believe it offers something different. Socially conservative, government limited to what it needs to do, and guided by our Heritage of Christian values.

I'm a chartered Chemical Engineer with a background in operations, design and software. This gives me a strong view on the coming man made energy crisis, which is driving up the cost of living.

Its also time we had accountability for the disaster that has befallen Woking Borough Council with its financial ruin. In the end, central government is responsible for the lending of that money and should adopt the debt.

Remember every vote counts and every vote gets counted. The result of this election may seem already preordained, but I assure you: who people vote for is very closely watched and noted.

Be clear on the signal you want to send.

Imprint: Promoted by R Squire on behalf of Tim Read, both of 12 Ockenden Close, Woking, GU22 7LU on behalf of Tim Read, both of the Heritage Party.

I have been a Councillor for over 10 years, representing Woodham, New Haw and Row Town at Surrey and Runnymede Borough Council. I am committed to community engagement, supporting local initiatives and ensuring residents’ voices are reflected in decision-making.
We have challenges in and around the ward and I am committed to making sure Woodham, Row Town and New Haw keeps moving. I have already tackled issues ranging from flooding, improvements to highways, approval for 3 pedestrian crossings, supported charities/community events plus tackled developments head on etc - always making sure residents' concerns are heard and not ignored.

Residents tell us all the time that we need to fix our roads. That’s why I’m standing with Scott Lewis to deliver real improvements for Woodham, Row Town, and New Haw. I’ve worked locally to support our community, listening to concerns on transport, safety, and protecting our green spaces.

Together, we’re focused on practical action resurfacing roads, improving crossings, and ensuring our area gets the attention it deserves.

This election is about strong local representation and getting things done. A vote for any other party puts progress at risk. Let’s keep our community moving forward with a team that will deliver.

I’m Ronan McCaughey and I’m asking people in Woodham, Row Town and New Haw to vote for me on 7 May because I care deeply about our community.

I believe in practical solutions, listening to residents, and standing up for what matters locally — from protecting services to supporting families and businesses. If elected, I will work hard, be visible, and always put our community first.

As someone who values family, community, and fairness, I want the new West Surrey Council to be a place where everyone feels supported and heard. I’ll bring energy, integrity, and a genuine commitment to making a positive difference. I’d be honoured to have your vote.

As a financial journalist, I’ll also be happy to ask tough questions about budgets, policies and decisions to ensure we get the best deal possible.

Chelsea has been a consistent volunteer and campaigner for local organisations, including volunteering full-time at the Runnymede food bank during the Covid-19 pandemic after losing her job because of Brexit. Seeing first hand the struggles that too many families face when trying to feed themselves re-affirmed Chelsea’s commitment to tackling the rising cost of living.

Now an adviser on public affairs and strategic communications, Chelsea has experience working with international businesses across a range of sectors including healthcare, transport, energy, manufacturing, legal and finance. Chelsea has a legal background and has worked at various levels of government, including at the European Parliament in Brussels. Understanding the globalised nature of business, she has long-campaigned for the UK’s position in Europe and at the heart of international debate.

As an avid rower in her free time, Chelsea is passionate about the Lib Dems’ policy to stop water companies dumping sewage, having witnessed the decline in our waterways over recent years. She is particularly concerned about the risk of harm to people and wildlife.

I'm proud to be running as your Labour & Co-op candidate for Runnymede’s local elections on the 2nd of May 2024.    I am a father of two, and a member of my children's school’s PTA. I have seen how austerity has impacted the local area and I want to be able to give something back to the community to support residents who’ve been let down during the Tory Cost-of-Living crisis.    Being there to support residents of Egham Hythe is what your Labour councillors Robert King & Rhys Davies have been doing and I want to support their vital work, especially as we have all been let down by the Conservative's years of financial mismanagement at Runnymede.

I have represented the residents of Worplesdon ward on Guildford Borough Council since being elected in 2023, and I currently serve as the Conservative Deputy Group Leader. Over the last three years, I have enjoyed supporting residents, tackling casework, and addressing local concerns with a practical, results-focused approach. I am now standing for the Worplesdon division in the proposed West Surrey Unitary Council so I can continue to serve our community.

My priorities are clear: protecting our valued green spaces, ensuring that any development is properly planned and supported by infrastructure, and resisting speculative building that does not meet local needs. I will work to improve road safety, support local services, and strengthen community facilities. I am also committed to improving support for children and families with special educational needs, ensuring they have access to the resources and opportunities they deserve. I will ensure the voices of our villages, Fairlands, Fox Corner, Jacobs Well, Normandy, Perry Hill, Pirbright, and Wanborough are heard while delivering sensible, balanced solutions for residents.

Jason Martin cares about local communities, helping vulnerable people, giving people a voice and opportunities. While others may dismiss certain ideas, policies or plans, Jason will communicate and think through them with the both people involved and those that it would effect to ensure that the best outcome happens.
He cares about young and vulnerable people, especially in these times, when they are at risk of being left behind or forgotten in council, Jason will give them a voice. He started a young LibDem movement in west Surrey which has quickly spread across the county and that’s given other young people the opportunity to run in this election with the Liberal Democrat’s.
He talks to people on the door and asks for their opinions, worries and concerns regardless of whether they’ll vote for him or not, because he knows that as long as they say what concern them, that it will be heard by someone and addressed.

Hello, my name is Alex and I am standing as a Green Party candidate because I believe they are the best option to represent our local areas here in Guildford and Waverley.

I have been based around Guildford for the past nine years. I came from a career as a Navy aircraft engineer and now work in HR. I love the beautiful scenery and all of the stunning walks here. I also enjoy playing board games and running Dungeons and Dragons games in my free time, including for a non-profit.

The Green Party have a long history of fighting to preserve our beautiful natural spaces but we also have a strong sense of moral justice.

**What I will work towards**

If elected I will:

- Hold water companies and sewage works to account. **The River Wey has seen sewage dumps nearly one hundred times.** That cannot go on and we need to monitor it closely
- Fight to protect our local assets from being sold into private ownership as a result of the huge debt we will inherit from the recent regional restructure
- Ensure we continue to invest in our community and our infrastructure
- Push for more regular and reliable bus services so everyone can get around without a car

**Why vote Green?**

A Green Party councillor is free to make choices in the best interest of their constituents and is not bound by party leadership like all of the other major parties. So, if you want someone to represent you and your community, vote Green!

I am standing because as a local I am fully aware of the issues that affect the lives of each of us every day and if elected I will do my part to address those most important to you.

I work in a special needs school and see firsthand how beneficial having the right resources in place can be for children and their families. I also study at Merrist Wood which is right here in the area and that connection to this community matters to me deeply.

**What I will work towards**

If elected I will:

- Work to ensure that SEND provisions in our schools are properly funded and supported. Parents of children with special educational needs should not have to fight tooth and nail for even small amounts of help. I will work to make sure that support is there where it is needed and adapted to the needs of each child and their family
- Seek to help our communities grow closer together again by tackling anti-social behaviour and providing more community initiatives and leisure options such as sports, arts and culture events
- Work to give greater support to our key workers
- Push to improve access to sustainable energy through green skills training and building the right infrastructure

I love this area and I want to play my part in making it the best it can be for everyone who lives here.
